pages.text: 84-25  February 25, 2025  RESOLUTION  AWARD 2015 USED OR NEW 30-PASSENGER FORD F550 SUPER DUTY CHASSIS  AND CAB JITNEY TYPE BUS OR EQUIVALENT  WHEREAS ,   the   West   Orange   Transportation   Division,   a   division   of   the   Department   of  Health,   requested   a   backup   Jitney-type   bus   due   to   the   increase   in   passengers,   supporting   the  Senior   Services   transportation   program,   the   aging   of   the   fleet,   and   the   recent   decommissioning   of  two buses due to lack of functionality, which necessitates the addition of more buses, and  WHEREAS ,   specifications   for   (1)   2015   model   used   or   new   30-passenger   Ford   F550  Super   Duty   Chassis   and   cab   Jitney   type   bus   or   equivalent   was   advertised   pursuant   to   the   New  Jersey Local Public Contracts Law (N. J. S. 40A:11-23); and  WHEREAS ,   at   the   date,   time,   and   place   advertised   for   the   opening   of   said   bids,   the  Township did receive three bids; and  WHEREAS   ,   the   Township   Purchasing   Agent   has   verified   that   the   successful   bidder   has  complied with the bid specifications and is the l owest responsible bidder; and  WHEREAS,   the   West   Orange   Department   of   Transportation   and   the   Assistant   Business  Administrator   have   reviewed   the   bids   and   are   satisfied   with   the   product   and   cost   and   have   further  considered   the   recommendation   of   the   Township   Purchasing   Agent   as   to   the   award   of   the   said  bid; and  NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   by   the   Municipal   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   purchase   of   One   (1)   2015   model   used   or   new   30-passenger   Ford   F550  Super   Duty   Chassis   and   cab   Jitney   type   bus   or   equivalent   is   hereby   awarded   to:   Ace   Limousine  located at 615 Wesley Avenue, Pleasantville, NJ 08232 in the amount of $55,000.00.  BE   IT   FURTHER   RESOLVED   that   the   Business   Administrator   is   hereby   authorized   to  arrange   to   pay   for   said   vehicle   with   the   terms   of   a   purchase   order   on   behalf   of   West   Orange   from  funds certified available by the Chief Financial Officer.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C., Municipal Clerk   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Adop ted: Feb ruary 25, 2025  I certify funds are available in Account No.  John C. Ditinyak, Chief Financial Officer

1  86-25  February 25, 20 25  RESOLUTION   AUTHORIZING TEMPORARY   CONSTRUCTION   EASEMENT   AND  PERMANENT ACCESS   AGREEMENT WITH   OWNER OF   73 7   NORTHFIELD AVENUE  WHEREAS ,   a   portion   of   the   Township ’ s   sanitary   sewer   line   runs   beneath   the   property  located   at   73 7   Northfield   Avenue ,   West   Orange,   NJ   07052,   known   as   Block   16 6 ,   Lot   14.01   (the  “ Property ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   has   determined   that   sanitary   and   stormwater   piping   extends   across   a  portion of the Property ; and  WHEREAS,   said   Temporary   and   Permanent   Easement   (the   “ Easement ” )   shall   include   the   right  to   mobilize,   use   the   area   for   staging   during   construction,   access,   excavate,   reconstruct   sanitary   and/or  storm water   piping   improvements   including   compaction,   grade,   backfill,   topsoil   and   seed   for   the   purposes  of   stabilizing   disturbed   areas,   preventing   erosion   and   restoring   GRANTOR ’ s   property   to   its   condition ;  and  WHEREAS ,   the   owner   of   the   Property   has   agreed   to   a   Deed   of   Temporary   Easement   and  Temporary Easement Agreement attached herein as Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Township   Engineer   has   deemed   this   Easement   necessary   to   protect   the   health  and safety of residents ; and  NOW,   BEING   HEREBY   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF   THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE,   that   Township   is   authorized   to   enter   into   the   Deed   of   Temporary  Easement and   Permanent Access   Agreement attached herein as Exhibit   “ A; ”   and   it is further  RESOLVED,   that   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law. DEED OF PROPOSED TEMPORARY CONSTRUCTION EASEMENT, PERMANENT ACCESS EASEMENT AND PERMANENT SANITARY AND STORM SEWER EASEMENT AGREEMENT  This   Deed   of   Temporary   Construction   Easement   and   Permanent   Easement Agreement made effective as of February   ____, 2025 BY AND BETWEEN ANITA THANI owner of   737 Northfield Avenue, Township of West Orange, County of Essex and State of New Jersey, hereinafter referred to as "GRANTOR," and THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE, a municipal corporation of the State of New Jersey, having offices located at 66 Main Street, in the Township of West Orange, County of Essex, and State of New Jersey, hereinafter referred to as the "GRANTEE” or the “TOWNSHIP.”  WITNESSETH  WHEREAS, the Grantor is the sole owner of Tax Block 166, Block 14.01 on the official Tax Map of the Township of West Orange, County of Essex, State and New Jersey, also known as 737 Northfield Avenue, West Orange, New Jersey (hereinafter the “PREMISES”), and  WHEREAS, the GRANTEE has determined that sanitary and stormwater piping extends across a portion of the Grantor’s property; and  WHEREAS, GRANTEE desires to obtain an easement on Grantor’s property to permit the Sanitary Sewer and/or Stormwater piping beneath the premises on Grantor’s property for the benefit of the general public and  WHEREAS, the GRANTOR is willing to grant to the GRANTEE and the GRANTEE is willing to accept a temporary construction, and permanent access easement and permanent sanitary and storm sewer easement over a portion of said premises that is to be an unobstructed permanent easement in order to construct, lay, maintain, operate, repair, alter, protect, replace and access sanitary and/or stormwater piping related thereto on the PREMISES; and  WHEREAS, the temporary construction easement and permanent access easement and   permanent   sanitary   and   storm   sewer   easement   are   shown   on   a   plan   entitled “EXISTING CONDITIONS SURVEY” prepared by Greenman-Pedersen Inc. (GPI) dated February 18, 2025 (“Exhibit A”) and set out in a Legal Description prepared by dated Greenman-Pedersen Inc. (GPI) dated February 18, 2025 (“Exhibit B”); and  Said Temporary and Permanent Easement shall include the right to mobilize, use the area for staging during construction, access, excavate, reconstruct sanitary and/or stormwater piping improvements including compaction, grade, backfill, topsoil and seed for the purposes of stabilizing disturbed areas, preventing erosion and restoring GRANTOR’s property to its condition; and

NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the benefits to accrue both community wide and to the GRANTEE, and for the general health, benefit and welfare of the community and otherwise, in further consideration of the covenants contained herein, in addition to One ($1.00) Dollar and other good and valuable consideration, GRANTOR hereby gives and grants and conveys to the GRANTEE, its successors and assigns.  The GRANTOR hereby covenants with the GRANTEE that:  a.   The GRANTOR has good and lawful right to convey the temporary construction easement, permanent access easement and permanent sanitary and storm sewer easement   reference herein.  b.   The temporary construction easement, permanent access easement and permanent sanitary and storm sewer easement   reference herein are for the sole use of GRANTEE; Grantee’s heirs, successors and assignees, and their invitees;  c.   The   temporary   and   permanent   easements   are   free   and   clear   from encumbrances of record that might interfere with the scope of work reference herein.  d.   The GRANTEE has a sanitary and/or stormwater sewer lines that extends across a portion of the GRANTOR’S property;  e.   GRANTEE will provide GRANTOR at least 48 hours’ notice prior to any work to maintain, repair and replace their sanitary and/or stormwater sewer facilities in, adjacent to, over or under the easement or any part thereof to be conducted in or on the easement unless such work is conducted on an emergency basis;  f.   GRANTOR does hereby expressly permit right of entry to the GRANTEE for the purposes of accessing the sanitary and/or stormwater is intended to be uninterrupted and unobstructed right of entry easement, under, across and over the area in “Exhibit A” and described in “Exhibit B” consisting of the right to erect, construct, install, use, impact, repair, remove, maintain, and preserve its sanitary and/or stormwater facilities;  g.   The Township of West Orange shall have quiet possession of the sanitary and/or stormwater sewer easement free from all encumbrances;  h.   GRANTOR, its successors and/or assigns shall hold harmless GRANTEE, its successors and/or assigns from and against any and all claim, liabilities, penalties, costs or other damages, whether foreseen and unforeseen, arising directly from the temporary and permanent easements reference herein.  i.   GRANTOR agrees that if any disturbance or damage is caused to the subject property as a result of the GRANTEE’S exercise of the rights and privileges afforded to it under this instrument, the GRANTEE shall restore the affected area as near as practicable to its original condition.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties have hereunder affixed their hands and seals the day and year first above written.  ____________________________  ANITA THANI  ATTESTED  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ______________________________   ________________________(seal)  Karen Carnevale, Township Clerk   by: Susan McCartney, Mayor  STATE OF NEW JERSEY  COUNTY OF ESSEX   ss:  I CERTIFY that on February ____, 2025  Anita Thani personally came before me and stated to my satisfaction that each person executed this instrument as his own act.  ______________________________

2889-25  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   AND   SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER   7,   TRAFFIC,  SUBSECTION   7-29.1,   SPEED   LIMITS,   GENERAL,   OF   THE   REVISED   GENERAL  ORDINANCES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE  BE   IT   ORDAINED ,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange,   in   the  County   of   Essex,   State   of   New   Jersey,   that   the   Speed   Limit   on   Kelly   Drive   a   Township   Road,   be  established   at   25   miles   per   hour ,   which   will   result   in   a   consistent   speed   limit   for   residential  neighborhood s   pursuant   to   N.J.A.C. 5:21-4.20   and N.J.S.A.   39:4- 8( b)   as   follows:  Section   1.   The   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   finds   it   in   the   interest  of   public   safety   to   establish   a   Speed   Limit   of   25   Miles   per   hour   on   K   e   l   l   y   D   r   i   v   e   between   Mount  Pleasant   Avenue   (N.J.   Route   10)   and   Haggerty   Drive,   a   Township   Road,   pursuant   to   N.J.A.C.   5:21-4.20  and   N.J.S.A.39:4-8 (b).  Section   2 .   Subsection   7-29.1,   Speed   Limits,   General,   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances  of   the   Township   of   West   Orange   is   hereby   supplemented   and   amended   as   follows   pursuant   to   the  provis ions   of N.J.S.A.   39:   4-197 (1a)   (italic   type   denotes   deletion,   bold   type   denotes   addition ) :  Name of Street   Direction   Speed Limit   Location  Kelly Drive   Both   25 MPH   From Mount Pleasan t  Avenue to Haggerty  Drive  Section   3.   If   any   provisions   of   the   Ordinance   or   application   thereof,   under   any  circumstances,   is   held   invalid,   the   invalidity   shall   not   affect   any   of   the   provisions   or   applications   of  this   Ordinance   that   can   be   given   effect   without   the   valid   provision(s)   or   application   and   to   this   end,  the   provisions   of   the   O rdinance are severable.  Section 4.   All   Other   Ordinances   or   parts   of   Ordinances   inconsistent   with   this   Ordinance

are hereby repealed.  Section 5.   Unless   another   penalty   is   expressly   provided   by   New   Jersey   Statute,   every  person   convicted   of   a   violation   of   any   provision   of   this   Ordinance   or   any   supplement   thereto   shall   be  liable   to   the   penalty   of   not   more   than   fifty   dollars   ($50.00)   or   imprisonment   for   a   term   not   to   exceed  fifteen (15)   days or both, as per W.O.R.G.O. Cha pter 7-4.2  Section 6.   Regulatory   and   warning   signs,   conforming   to   the   current   Manual   on   Uniform  Traffic   Control   Devices   (MUTCD),   shall   be   erected   and   maintained   to   effect   the   above-designated   speed  limit.  Susan Mc Cartney, Mayor  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Introduced : January 28, 2025  Adopted : February 11, 2025

2889-25  Legislative History  The Ordinance amends the Traffic Regulations of the Township, and establishes a Speed Limit of 25 miles  per hour (MPH) on Kelley Drive. Based on local residents ’   requests and Stat Track data provided by the  Traffic Division of the West Orange Police Department, the Municipal Engineer determined the need for  red uced traffic speeds in the area.

1  2890 - 25  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING AND SUPPLEMENTING CHAPTER   25   OF THE  REVISED   GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  BA NNING “POP - UP” PARTIES  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF   WEST  ORANGE, NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   25   hereby is and amended and supplemented to add a new section  governing “pop - up” parties as defined herein within the Township of West Orange :  §   1 — Scope and Purpose :  a.   The Township of West Orange (the “Township”) has received numerous complaints from  residents in various neighborhoods throughout the Township regarding parties, events, and/or  gatherings wherein the attendees of the events have purchased tickets or wristbands for entry into  residential homes within the Township. The purpose of this Section is to provide recourse for the  Township   if   such “pop - up” parties (defined herein)   occur in the future and to put the public on  notice that the same are prohibited as a health and safety concern for the general welfare of the  public .  §   2 — Definitions:  “Advertisement”   shall be defined for the purposes of this Ordinance to include the  creation of any flyer, social media posting, text message, or other form of electronic   or  other   communication soliciting guests to a residential home for payment of any amount.  “Pop - Up Party(ies)”   shall be defined as   a party   including   any event and/or gathering  occurring   in a residential home,   apartment   or   other   location (excluding commercial  establishments such as   licensed restaurants, bars, or other event halls where such activity is  permitted ) whereby the owner, occupant and/or operator of the residence solicits   or  advertises   the   use   of   the ir   residence for an event, party, or gathering   and where the guests  are subject to payment for tickets, wristbands, or other mode of entry into the residence , or  for food, alcoholic or non - alcoholic beverages.  §   3 — Prohibitions:  a.   Any person, owner, occupant, or operator of a residence located in the residential zoning  district of the Township of West Orange shall be prohibited from advertising or otherwise  soliciting monies for entry into their home , food, or beverages   to attend any Pop - Up Party  as defined herein.  b.   Any person who rents or otherwise loans their property in the residential district of the  Township of West Orange to a person for a Pop - Up Party is   in violation of this Ordinance.  c.   Any person who creates, submits, or otherwise posts an Advertisement for a Pop - Up Party  shall be considered   in violation   of this Ordinance   and the same shall be considered a  separate violation under this Ordinance,   subject to the penalties provided for herein.  d.   Exemptions: Activities, gatherings, or other events that may constitute a Pop - Up Party  under this Ordinance shall be exempted where the proceeds for the same shall be used for  Deleted:   ( for   profit )

2  any   bona fide   charitable organization licensed in the State of New Jersey and/or for  political contributions to any   bona fide   local, state, or federal campaign   which has been  properly registered with the appropriate authority .  §   4 — Enforcement  a.   The Township Police Department,   the Township Health Department,   the Township Zoning  Official, the Township Fire Department,   their   agents, employees, officers, or assigns shall  have the power to enforce the provisions of this Ordinance.  §   5 — Penalties:  a.   All penalties for violation of any provision of this section shall be governed by the  provisions of Section   1 - 5   et seq. of the Revised General Ordinances of the Township of  West Orange.  §   6 — Severability:  Each section, subsection, sentence, clause and phrase of this Ordinance is declared to be an  independent section, subsection, sentence, clause and phrase, and the finding or holding of  any such portion of this Ordinance to be unconstitutional, void, or ineffective for any cause,  or reason, shall not affect any other portion of this Ordinance.  § 7 — Repealer.  All other ordinances or parts of ordinances inconsistent with this section are hereby  repealed.  § 8 — Effective Date:  This Ordinance shall be in full force and effect from and after its adoption and any  publication as required by law.  Susan McCartney, Mayor  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak, Council President  Municipal Clerk  Introduced   : January 28, 2025  Adopted   : February 11, 2025

3  2890 - 25  Legislative History  The Township of West Orange (the “Township”) has received numerous complaints from  residents in various neighborhoods throughout the Township regarding parties, events, and/or  gatherings wherein the attendees of the events have purchased tickets or wristbands for entry into  residential homes within the Township.   The purpose of this Section is to provide recourse for  the Township if such “pop - up” parties occur in the future and to put the public on notice that the  same are prohibited as a health and safety concern for the general welfare of the public.

2892 - 2 5  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   2 , S E CTION S   57.1,   57 . 2 , and 57.6   GENERAL  ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( OPEN SPACE AND RECREATION COMMITTEE AND TRUST FUND )  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE, NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   2, Section s   57.1,   57 . 2 , and 57.6   o f the Revised  General   Ordinances of the Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is   to   modify the   composition and terms for the membership  on the Township of West Orange’s Open Space and Recreation Commi ttee   including the  removal of two members   currently   appoint ed   by the Mayor and the addition of one additional  appointment by the Township Council to be made as a consensus selection by the vote of the  majority of the Township Council.   In addition, this ordinance   modifies   all terms on the  Committee   to   one - year   terms, but allow s   for re - appointments .   Also, this ordinance   requires the  Municipal Clerk to post meeting minutes to the Township website.  I I .   CHAPTER   2 SECTION 57. 1   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  2 - 57. 1   Establishment of Open Space and Recreation Committee .  There is hereby established an Open Space and Recreation Committee which shall consist of  seven   members whose terms and functions are hereinafter set forth  I I I .   CHAPTER   2 SECTION   57 . 2   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  2 - 57 . 2   Membership and Appointment; Term .  Each member of the Open Space and Recreation Committee shall be a resident of the Township  of West   Orange . One   member   shall be appointed by the Mayor .   F ive   members   shall be appointed  by the Township   Council   with   each Council   M ember selecting one appointee .   .   Annually, as  early   e ach year   as practica b le ,   the Council shall   s elect one   addition al   member by majority vot e  to serve as a member of the Committee.   A ll   members   shall serve without compensation   and for a  term of one year unless the Mayor or Council Member(s) of the Township Council appointing  that member ends his or her term   prio r to December 31 of any particular year   which shall result  in a vacancy for the remainder of that year .   A ny   vacancy occurring   shall be filled for the  unexpired term only   in the same manner as the vacating member was appointed .   All members  may be reappointed to the Committee .  I V .   CHAPTER   2 SECTIONS 57.6   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  2 - 57.6   Rules of Order and Minutes . 2  For the purpose of conducting its meetings in an orderly fashion, the Open Space and Recreation  Committee shall make reasonable efforts to use and follow the most recent edition of Robert's  Rules of Order.   On an annual basis   as early in the year as practicable ,   t he Committee shall  select from among its members a   Chair person   of the Open Space and Recreation Committee,  who shall preside over all meetings   and a   Vice Chair p erson   to serve as the presiding officer in  the absence of the Chair person . The Committee shall also select a Secretary whose function shall  be to keep reasonably comprehensive minutes of the Committee's meetings, showing the time,  place, the members present, the subjects considered, actions taken, the vote of each member and  other appropriate information pertaining to the proceedings of the Committee. Minutes shall be  kept of every meeting and a copy shall be sent to the Municipal Clerk promptly after their  adoption by the Committee.   The Municipal Clerk shall keep and maintain   such minutes in the  Clerk’s office available for review by the public.   The Municipal Clerk will also cause such  minutes to be promptly and regularly posted on the Township website on the website page  reserved for information on the Open Space and Recreation Committee. 3  2892 - 25  Legislative History  The purpose of this ordinance is   to modify the composition and terms for the membership  on the Township of West Orange’s Open Space and Recreation Committee including the  removal of two members currently appointed by the Mayor and the addition of one additional  appointment by the Township Council to be made as a consensus selection by the vote of the  majority of the Township Council.   In addition, this ordinance modifies all terms on the  Committee to   one - year   terms, but allows for re - appointments .   This ordinance also requires that  the minutes be posted on the Township Open Space and Recreation website page.

2894 - 2 5  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   2 , S E CTION   9. 4   GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( SPECIAL COUNSEL )  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE,   NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   2,   Section s   9. 4   o f   the Revised General  Ordinances of the Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is   to   modify the   provisions of the Township of West  Orange   Municipal   Code   governing   the   Township’s   retention   of   Special   Counsel .   This  ordinance: (i) requires appointment of Special Counsel by advice and consent of the Township  Council; and (ii) require that the bills for Special Counsel shall be subject to approval from the  Township Council.  I I .   CHAPTER   2 SECTION S   9.4   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  2 - 9. 4   Special Counsel .  Subject to   the advi c e   and consent of the   Township Council , the Township Attorney may appoint  such Special Counsel as may be required or advisable to handle matters in which a conflict of  interest precludes representation by the Township Attorney or Assistant Township Attorney, or  which due to its complexity, special nature, time demands or constraints, or for any good reason  would benefit from the special experience and/or training of the Special Counsel to be hired.  Such Special Counsel need not serve full time and would be expected to serve for the duration of  the matter for which he or she was appointed, subject to annual reappointment with the manner  and amount of compensation to be approved by the   Township Council . Special Counsel would  be subject to the supervision of the Township Attorney   except for matters   involving a conflict of  interest precluding representation by the Township Attorney wherein   Special Counsel would be  subject to the supervision of the   Assistant Township Attorney   or designated conflict counsel . 2897-25  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   will   prohibit   left   turns   from   Glenview   Drive   southbound   to   Northfield   Avenue   eastbound  during   all   hours.   This   Prohibition   was   requested   by   the   West   Orange   Police   Traffic   Division   after   two  recent   auto/pedestrian   accidents.   There   is   an   unregulated   pedestrian   crossing   at   this   intersection   that  allows   residents   of   the   neighborhood   to   cross   Northfield   to   the   eastbound   side   to   access   the   bus   stop.  This Ordinance is designed to make pedestrian safety a priority.

2899-25  Legisl a tive History  The   Ordinance,   which   amends   the   Traffic   Regulations   of   the   Township,   designates   various   types  of   new   Stop   intersection s   with   Stop   s igns   on   the   nam ed   approaches .   Based   on   recommendations  from   the   West   Orange   Police   Traffic   Division   and   residents ’   requests,   the   Municipal   Engineer  designated   Bright   View   Engineering   of   Roseland   to   conduct   the   necessary   traffic   studies   and  assemble   data   to   comply   with   N.J.S.A.   39:4-8   regulations.   Additionally,   two   stop   intersections  created during new development of the Colonial Woo ds Drive are being added as well.

70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   P.O. Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  February 4, 2025  VIA EMAIL  Zayibeth Carballo, PE, PLS, PP, CME, CFM, CFS  Director, Municipal Engineer  Department of Engineering  Township of West Orange  25 Lakeside Avenue  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  Re:   Safety Study  Cullen Drive & Carlson Road  West Orange Township, Essex County, New Jersey  BVE Project No.: 241193  Dear Ms. Carballo:  Bright View Engineering was tasked by the Township of West Orange ( the Township here-in )  with performing an evaluation of the existing traffic controls at the intersection of Cullen Drive &  Carlson Road. This analysis was performed to assess the safety conditions at the intersection and  the installation of a stop control on Cullen Drive.  Bright View Engineering ( BVE here-in ), in coordination with the Township, collected data during  various site visits, reviewed crash information provided by the Township and reviewed data  provided by NJDOT’s Safety Voyager to assess the existing conditions at the study location. The  collected data and observations were utilized to determine if the existing traffic conditions in the  project area justify the implementation of the new traffic pattern. Our methodology conforms with  the provisions set forth in NJSA   Title 39:4-8 , Institute of Transportation Engineers ( ITE ) literature,  and the applicable requirements of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices ( MUTCD ).  The following points were considered within the scope of our study:     An inventory of the roadway facilities in the project vicinity, including the existing physical  and traffic operating characteristics;     Crash data along roadway segments interconnecting the study intersections;     Recommendations;     Summary and Conclusions.

Safety Study  Cullen Drive & Carlson Road  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 2 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  EXISTING CONDITIONS  Multiple   field investigations   were   conducted to   obtain   an inventory   of existing   roadway  conditions, posted traffic controls, adjacent land uses, lane configurations of the roadways in the  study area, and existing vehicular and pedestrian traffic patterns. The following is a brief  description of the roadways:  The intersection of Cullen Drive & Carlson Road is a 3-legged unsignalized intersection. There is  no traffic control at the intersection. We note that the intersection is approximately 600 feet from  Mount Pleasant Elementary School.  Carlson Road   is an east to west local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West Orange  which spans 0.14 miles between Ellison Avenue and Colonial Woods Drive. There are no NJDOT  Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. There is no speed limit posted along the  roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance  set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Carlson Road provides an approximate pavement  width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street  parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Carlson Road is generally  residential in nature.  Cullen Drive   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West Orange  which spans 0.22 miles between Carlson Road and a dead end. There are no NJDOT Straight-Line  Diagrams available for this roadway. There is no speed limit posted along the roadway. It is our  opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth within  the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Cullen Drive provides an approximate pavement width of 30-ft  and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street parking is  permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Cullen Drive is generally residential  in nature.

Safety Study  Cullen Drive & Carlson Road  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 4 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  DATA COLLECTION  CRASH HISTORY DATA  BVE obtained crash data from the Township and through NJDOT’s Safety Voyager. There were  no crashes reported at the study intersection within a five-year period.  FIELD OBSERVATIONS  BVE conducted field data collection and performed vehicle runs along the study area. We observed  that presently, there are no traffic controls at the study intersection. The study area is a residential  neighborhood with primarily single-family housing. This analysis was performed to assess the safety conditions at the  intersection and the installation of a stop control on Nottingham Road.  Bright View Engineering ( BVE here-in ), in coordination with the Township, collected data during  various site visits, reviewed crash information provided by the Township and reviewed data  provided by NJDOT’s Safety Voyager to assess the existing conditions at the study location. The  collected data and observations were utilized to determine if the existing traffic conditions in the  project area justify the implementation of the new traffic pattern. Our methodology conforms with  the provisions set forth in NJSA   Title 39:4-8 , Institute of Transportation Engineers ( ITE ) literature,  and the applicable requirements of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices ( MUTCD ).  The following points were considered within the scope of our study:     An inventory of the roadway facilities in the project vicinity, including the existing physical  and traffic operating characteristics;     Crash data along roadway segments interconnecting the study intersections;     Recommendations;     Summary and Conclusions.

Safety Study  Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 2 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  EXISTING CONDITIONS  Multiple   field investigations   were   conducted to   obtain   an inventory   of existing   roadway  conditions, posted traffic controls, adjacent land uses, lane configurations of the roadways in the  study area, and existing vehicular and pedestrian traffic patterns. The following is a brief  description of the roadways:  The   intersection   of   Devonshire   Terrace   &   Nottingham   Road   is   a   3-legged   unsignalized  intersection. There is no traffic control at the intersection.  Devonshire Terrace   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.18 miles between Nottingham Road and Mount Pleasant Avenue. There are  no NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. Devonshire Terrace is STOP-  controlled at its intersection with Mount Pleasant Avenue. There is no speed limit posted along the  roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance  set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Devonshire Terrace provides an approximate  pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach.  On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Devonshire  Terrace is generally residential in nature.  Nottingham Road   is an east to west local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.14 miles between Normandy Terrace and Devonshire Terrace. There are no  NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. There is no speed limit posted along  the roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the  guidance set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Nottingham Road provides an  approximate pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for  each approach. On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along  Nottingham Road is generally residential in nature.

Safety Study  Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 4 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  DATA COLLECTION  CRASH HISTORY DATA  BVE obtained crash data from the Township and through NJDOT’s Safety Voyager. There were  no crashes reported at the study intersection within a five-year period.  FIELD OBSERVATIONS  BVE conducted field data collection and performed vehicle runs along the study area. We observed  that presently, there are no traffic controls at the study intersection. The study area is a residential  neighborhood with primarily single-family housing.  We observed that the horizontal and vertical sight lines at the intersection of Devonshire Terrace  & Nottingham Road are obstructed due to overgrown foliage and roadway’s grade. Nottingham  Road has an uphill grade as approaching the intersection.  FIGURE 2 – Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road

Safety Study  Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 5 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  RECOMMENDATIONS  It is our recommendation that a STOP sign be installed on the Nottingham Road approach at the  study intersection. Treatments which can be considered include but are not limited to:  Sight Distance Improvements –   At the intersection of Devonshire Terrace & Nottingham Road,  the line of sight of approaching vehicles was found to be obstructed due to foliage and the  intersection’s geometry. We observed that if vehicles stop closer to the mainline on Devonshire  Terrace, then they would have clear visibility of the traffic conditions at the intersection and would  be able to safely make a turn onto Devonshire Terrace.  It is our opinion that if the tree and other foliage at the corners of the intersection are cleared, then  the sight distance can be improved. We recommend that the Township cooperate with its residents  to trim overgrown foliage and clear obstruction from sight triangles, as reasonable.  FIGURE 3 – Intersection Sight Distance

The STOP sign would  provide the appropriate traffic control at the study intersection to improve safety and circulation  of traffic. We recommend that the Township install a STOP sign on Nottingham Road at the study  intersection. The installation of the STOP sign should be implemented per the guidance presented  within Chapter 2B-Regulatory Signs, Barricades, and Gates of the MUTCD (11 th   Edition).  FIGURE 4 – STOP Sign Installation  Striping and Signages –   Given the wide curb radius at the study intersection and roadway  geometry, we recommend the implementation of additional striping to support the installation of  the stop sign. We recommend that the Township provide a stop bar and double yellow line striping  on Nottingham Road to support the stop sign.  Sidewalk and Crosswalk Markings   – We recommend that the Township consider installing  sidewalks and marked crosswalks within the study area to improve pedestrian safety and  circulation. The implementation of the roadway striping, and signs should follow the guidance  presented within the latest edition of the MUTCD.

Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  February 4, 2025  VIA EMAIL  Zayibeth Carballo, PE, PLS, PP, CME, CFM, CFS  Director, Municipal Engineer  Department of Engineering  Township of West Orange  25 Lakeside Avenue  West Orange, New Jersey 07052  Re:   Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  West Orange Township, Essex County, New Jersey  BVE Project No.: 241193  Dear Ms. Carballo:  Bright View Engineering was tasked by the Township of West Orange ( the Township here-in )  with performing an evaluation of the existing traffic controls at the intersection of Nottingham  Road & Normandy Terrace. This analysis was performed to assess the safety conditions at the  intersection and the installation of a stop control on Nottingham Road.  Bright View Engineering ( BVE here-in ), in coordination with the Township, collected data during  various site visits, reviewed crash information provided by the Township and reviewed data  provided by NJDOT’s Safety Voyager to assess the existing conditions at the study location. The  collected data and observations were utilized to determine if the existing traffic conditions in the  project area justify the implementation of the new traffic pattern. Our methodology conforms with  the provisions set forth in NJSA   Title 39:4-8 , Institute of Transportation Engineers ( ITE ) literature,  and the applicable requirements of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices ( MUTCD ).  The following points were considered within the scope of our study:     An inventory of the roadway facilities in the project vicinity, including the existing physical  and traffic operating characteristics;     Crash data along roadway segments interconnecting the study intersections;     Recommendations;     Summary and Conclusions.

Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 2 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  EXISTING CONDITIONS  Multiple   field investigations   were   conducted to   obtain   an inventory   of existing   roadway  conditions, posted traffic controls, adjacent land uses, lane configurations of the roadways in the  study area, and existing vehicular and pedestrian traffic patterns. The following is a brief  description of the roadways:  The intersection of Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace is a 3-legged unsignalized intersection.  There is no traffic control at the intersection. We note that striping for a Stop control has been  installed at the intersection along Nottingham Road.  Normandy Terrace   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.15 miles between Buckingham Road and Manchester Road. There are no  NJDOT Straight-Line   Diagrams   available   for this roadway.   Normandy Terrace is STOP-  controlled at its intersection with Buckingham Road. There is no speed limit posted along the  roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance  set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Normandy Terrace provides an approximate  pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach.  On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Normandy  Terrace is generally residential in nature.  Nottingham Road   is an east to west local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.14 miles between Normandy Terrace and Devonshire Terrace. There are no  NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. There is no speed limit posted along  the roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the  guidance set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Nottingham Road provides an  approximate pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for  each approach. On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along  Nottingham Road is generally residential in nature.

Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 4 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  DATA COLLECTION  CRASH HISTORY DATA  BVE obtained crash data from the Township and through NJDOT’s Safety Voyager. There were  no crashes reported at the study intersection within a five-year period.  FIELD OBSERVATIONS  BVE conducted field data collection and performed vehicle runs along the study area. We observed  that presently, there are no traffic controls at the study intersection. The study area is a residential  neighborhood with primarily single-family housing.  We observed that the sight lines at the intersection of Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace are  obstructed due to overgrown foliage. We note that striping for a Stop control has been installed at  the intersection along Normandy Terrace.  FIGURE 2 – Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  NORMANDY   TERRACE

Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 5 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  RECOMMENDATIONS  It is our recommendation that a STOP sign be installed on the Nottingham Road approach at the  study intersection. Treatments which can be considered include but are not limited to:  Sight Distance Improvements –   At the intersection of Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace,  the line of sight of approaching vehicles was found to be obstructed. We observed that if vehicles  stop closer to the mainline on Normandy Terrace, then they would have clear visibility of the  traffic conditions at the intersection and would be able to safely make a turn onto Normandy  Terrace.  It is our opinion that if the tree and other foliage at the corners of the intersection are cleared, then  the sight distance can be improved. We recommend that the Township cooperate with its residents  to trim overgrown foliage and clear obstruction from sight triangles, as reasonable.  FIGURE 3 – Intersection Sight Distance

Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 6 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  STOP Sign Installation –   A STOP control on the minor-road approach should be considered as  per Section 2B.11-Minor Road Stop Control of the MUTCD (11 th   Edition). The STOP sign would  provide the appropriate traffic control at the study intersection to improve safety and circulation  of traffic. We recommend that the Township install a STOP sign on Nottingham Road at the study  intersection. The installation of the STOP sign should be implemented per the guidance presented  within Chapter 2B-Regulatory Signs, Barricades, and Gates of the MUTCD (11 th   Edition).  FIGURE 4 – STOP Sign Installation  Sidewalk and Crosswalk Markings   – We recommend that the Township maintain their  sidewalks,   install   additional   sidewalks   along   Nottingham   Road,   and   update   their   marked  crosswalks within the study area to improve pedestrian safety and circulation. The implementation  of the roadway striping, and signs should follow the guidance presented within the latest edition  of the MUTCD.  NORMANDY TERRACE

Safety Study  Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 7 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  SUMMARY & CONCLUSIONS  It is our recommendation, based upon our field observations, the guidance from MUTCD and Title  39, and the existing conditions, that the Township of West Orange should consider the installation  of a STOP sign at the intersection of Nottingham Road & Normandy Terrace. There is no speed limit posted along the roadway. It is  our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth  within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Lancaster Terrace provides an approximate pavement  width of 32-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street  parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Lancaster Terrace is  generally residential in nature.  Manchester Road   is an east to west local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.10 miles between Westminster Terrace and Lancaster Terrace. There are no  NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. There is no speed limit posted along  the roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the  guidance set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Manchester Road provides an  approximate pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for  each approach. On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along  Manchester Road is generally residential in nature.

This analysis was performed to assess the safety conditions at the  intersection and the installation of a stop control on Brook Place.  Bright View Engineering ( BVE here-in ), in coordination with the Township, collected data during  various site visits, reviewed crash information provided by the Township and reviewed data  provided by NJDOT’s Safety Voyager to assess the existing conditions at the study location. The  collected data and observations were utilized to determine if the existing traffic conditions in the  project area justify the implementation of the new traffic pattern. Our methodology conforms with  the provisions set forth in NJSA   Title 39:4-8 , Institute of Transportation Engineers ( ITE ) literature,  and the applicable requirements of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices ( MUTCD ).  The following points were considered within the scope of our study:     An inventory of the roadway facilities in the project vicinity, including the existing physical  and traffic operating characteristics;     Crash data along roadway segments interconnecting the study intersections;     Recommendations;     Summary and Conclusions.

Safety Study  Brook Place & Mount Pleasant Place  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 2 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  EXISTING CONDITIONS  Multiple   field investigations   were   conducted to   obtain   an inventory   of existing   roadway  conditions, posted traffic controls, adjacent land uses, lane configurations of the roadways in the  study area, and existing vehicular and pedestrian traffic patterns. The following is a brief  description of the roadways:  The intersection of Brook Place & Mount Pleasant Place is a 3-legged unsignalized intersection.  There is no traffic control at the intersection. We note that the intersection is approximately 400  feet away from West Orange Fire Station #5 on Mount Pleasant Place.  Brook Place   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West Orange  which spans 0.20 miles between Buckingham Road and Mount Pleasant Place. There are no  NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. Brock Place is STOP-controlled at its  intersection with Buckingham Road. There is no speed limit posted along the roadway. It is our  opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth within  the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Brook Place provides an approximate pavement width of 30-ft  and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street parking is  permitted along both sides of the roadway. The land use along Brook Place is generally residential  in nature.  Mount Pleasant Place   is an east to west local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of  West Orange which spans 0.20 miles between Pleasant Valley Way and Mount Pleasant Avenue.  There are no NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. Mount Pleasant Place is  STOP-controlled at its intersection with Pleasant Valley Way and Mount Pleasant Avenue. There  is no speed limit posted along the roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is  appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39.  Mount Pleasant Place provides an approximate pavement width of 27-ft and allows for bi-  directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street parking is permitted along both  sides of the roadway. The land use along Mount Pleasant Place is generally residential in nature  with businesses near Mount Pleasant Avenue.

This analysis was performed to assess the safety conditions at the intersection and  the installation of a stop control on Devon Drive.  Bright View Engineering ( BVE here-in ), in coordination with the Township, collected data during  various site visits, reviewed crash information provided by the Township and reviewed data  provided by NJDOT’s Safety Voyager to assess the existing conditions at the study location. The  collected data and observations were utilized to determine if the existing traffic conditions in the  project area justify the implementation of the new traffic pattern. Our methodology conforms with  the provisions set forth in NJSA   Title 39:4-8 , Institute of Transportation Engineers ( ITE ) literature,  and the applicable requirements of the Manual on Uniform Traffic Control Devices ( MUTCD ).  The following points were considered within the scope of our study:     An inventory of the roadway facilities in the project vicinity, including the existing physical  and traffic operating characteristics;     Crash data along roadway segments interconnecting the study intersections;     Recommendations;     Summary and Conclusions.

Safety Study  Devon Drive & Mountain Way  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 2 of 9  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  EXISTING CONDITIONS  Multiple   field investigations   were   conducted to   obtain   an inventory   of existing   roadway  conditions, posted traffic controls, adjacent land uses, lane configurations of the roadways in the  study area, and existing vehicular and pedestrian traffic patterns. The following is a brief  description of the roadways:  The intersection of Devon Drive & Mountain Way is a four-way unsignalized intersection. There  is no traffic control at the intersection.  Devon Drive   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West Orange  which spans 0.20 miles. There are no NJDOT Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway.  There is no speed limit posted along the roadway. It is our opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is  appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth within the New Jersey Statutes Title 39.  Devon Drive provides an approximate pavement width of 30-ft and allows for bi-directional travel  with one travel lane for each approach. On-street parking is permitted along both sides of the  roadway. The land use along Devon Drive is generally residential in nature.  Mountain Way   is a north to south local street under the jurisdiction of the Township of West  Orange which spans 0.28 miles between Walker Road and Lowell Avenue. There are no NJDOT  Straight-Line Diagrams available for this roadway. Mountain Way is STOP-controlled at its  intersection with Walker Road. The speed limit on the roadway is posted at 25 MPH. It is our  opinion that a 25 MPH speed limit is appropriate and conforms with the guidance set forth within  the New Jersey Statutes Title 39. Mountain Way provides an approximate pavement width of 30-  ft and allows for bi-directional travel with one travel lane for each approach. On-street parking is  permitted along both sides of the roadway.   The land use along Mountain Way is generally  residential in nature.

FIGURE 4 – STOP Sign Installation  Striping and Signages –   Given the wide curb radius at the study intersection, we recommend the  implementation of additional striping to support the installation of the stop sign. We recommend  that the Township provide a stop bar and double yellow line striping on Sylvan Place to support  the stop sign.  Sidewalk and Crosswalk Markings   – We recommend that the Township maintain their  sidewalks and update their marked crosswalks within the study area to improve pedestrian safety  and circulation. The implementation of the roadway striping, and signs should follow the guidance  presented within the latest edition of the MUTCD.  BURNETT TERRACE

Safety Study  Sylvan Place & Burnett Terrace  Project No.: 241193  February 4, 2025  Page 7 of 7  70 South Orange Avenue, Suite 109   PO Box 99  Livingston, New Jersey 07039   Roseland, NJ 07068  C: (732) 236-7557 T: (973) 228-0999 F: (201) 753-3904  BrightViewEngineering.com  SUMMARY & CONCLUSIONS  It is our recommendation, based upon our field observations, the guidance from MUTCD and Title  39, and the existing conditions, that the Township of West Orange should consider the installation  of a STOP sign and additional striping at the intersection of Sylvan Place & Burnett Terrace.  Should you have any questions or require additional information please do not hesitate to contact  me at (732) 236-7557.  Very truly yours,  Bright View Engineering  John Jahr, P.T.P., T.S.O.S. 2900 - 25  AN   ORDINANCE   AMENDING   AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER 28   OF THE  REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE   TO  ADD A SECTION AND ARTICLE REGARDING USAGE OF LEAF BLOWERS  WITHIN THE TOWNSHIP  BE   IT ORDAINED BY THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE, NEW JERSEY , that Chapter 28 of the Revised General Ordinances of the  Township of West Orange be and is hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  This   purpose of this   Ordinance   is   to limit the use of gas - powered leaf blowers within the  Township of West Orange and to promote a transition to the exclusive use of electric and  battery - operated blowers on all properties within the Township whether privately or Township -  owned.  ARTICLE II: USAGE OF LEAF BLOWERS WITHIN THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE  § 28 - 12 — DEFINITIONS  Air Quality Action Day : An air quality action day is called when the ozone and/or particulate  matter forecasts reach an Air Quality Index (AQI) of orange (unhealthy for sensitive groups), red  (unhealthy), or maroon (very unhealthy) .  Electric - Powered Leaf Blower:   A device powered by a battery or plug - in electricity, used in  yard maintenance.  Gas - Powered Leaf Blower:   A device powered by a two or four stroke engine using gasoline or  a gasoline - oil mixture, primarily used for blowing grass clippings, debris, leaves, and dirt in yard  and lawn maintenance.  Leaf Blowers : Gas or electric - powered leaf blowers.  Turbine Blower:   (commonly referred to as a "Buffalo Blower"): A piece of equipment attached  to a small tractor, golf cart, or other vehicle, and shall only be permitted pursuant to this  ordinance only golf courses.  Lot : A designated parcel tract of land established in the record as of local and county registers  of deed by plat, deed, subdivision, zoning lot of record, or as otherwise permitted by law.  § 28 - 13 — LIMITATION ON LEAF BLOWERS  The use of gas - powered leaf blowers, except for turbine blowers, is prohibited within the  Township of West Orange on residential, commercial, and Township - owned properties, subject  to the following conditions:

2  1.   Gas - powered leaf blowers may be used from April 15 to May 15, 2025, and from  October 30 to December 15, 2025. Effective January 1, 2026, the use of gas - powered  blowers shall be prohibited throughout the Township.  2.   Leaf blowers shall not be used for the application of chemicals or products to treat insect  or pest infestations.  3.   Gas - powered blowers shall not be used for gutter cleaning or snow clearing.  4.   No gas or diesel generators may be utilized to power any electric leaf blowers at any  time.  5.   Leaf blowers shall not be operated on designated “air quality action days" as designated  by the New Jersey Department of Environmental Protection and declared by the  Township’s Emergency Management Coordinator.  6.   The hours of operation for all leaf blowers shall be limited to:  Weekdays: 8am to 5pm  Weekends: 10am. to 5pm.  7.   The use of turbine blowers shall be restricted to lots in excess of 15 acres. The  prohibition of using turbine blowers for the application of chemicals to treat bug or insect  infestation is banned.  §28 - 14 — EMERGENCIES  The   Emergency Management Coordinator   has the authority to suspend one or more  provisions of this chapter for up to 24 hours if they determine an emergency exists in the  Township. This suspension can be extended daily for as long as the emergency continues.  §28 - 15 — ENFORCEMENT  This ordinance will be enforced by the   Zoning Official and/or their   designee . To report a  violation, individuals should email ZoningOfficial@westorange.org with a time - stamped photo  showing the violation, along with any relevant details such as equipment, company truck, and the  incident's address, date, and time. The Zoning   Official and/or their designee   will then issue a  summons to both the homeowner and the landscaper. If the summons is disputed or not paid, a  court date will be scheduled.  Penalties for violations:  ●   First Violation: Written warning issued to both homeowner and landscaping company  ●   Second Violation: $200 fine issued to both homeowner and landscaping company  ●   Third Violation: $500 fine issued to both homeowner and landscaping company  After three violations by the same landscaping company, that company shall be suspended from  working in West Orange for one year. Each violation shall be considered a separate offense.  Deleted:   Code Enforcement Officer  Deleted:   Officer

3  § 28 - 16 — LAWN CARE REGISTRY  To ensure compliance with this ordinance, all lawn care businesses operating within the  Township must be registered with the Township.   A list of authorized businesses will be posted  on the Township’s website . This registry will be formalized by resolution.  II.   SEVERABILITY  If any part of this Ordinance is found to be invalid, the judgment will apply only to that  specific section, paragraph, subparagraph, clause, or provision, and   the remainder of the  Ordinance will remain valid and in effect.  III .   REPEAL OF   CONFLICTING   ORDINANCES  All ordinances or parts of ordinances inconsistent with this chapter are hereby repealed.  IV .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication, as provided by law.  SUSAN McCARTNEY, MAYOR  JOE KRAKOVIAK ,  COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.,  MUNICIPAL CLERK  INTRODUCED:  ADOPTED:

4  Legislative History  S cien c e has shown that the noise, particulate matter, gas and oil fuel mixture being  released unburned, and the fumes from burnt fuel are hazardous to the health of the workers,  residents, particularly seniors and children and those with respiratory conditions, causing hearing  loss and potential cancers, hypertension, cardiac disease, and increased stress levels .   The  practice   of   blowing   leaves   and   other   debris   for   property   maintenance   has   unintended  environmental   and   health   consequences   that   disrupt   and   destroy   animal,   bird,   insect   and  pollinator habitat, including adversely impacting the respiratory health of our pets .   The  Township wishes to continue to strengthen its commitment to public health and wellness and  environmental sustainability and believes that the proposed amendments will help achieve  greater equity and public health as well as quality of life .   C ommercial - grade battery - powered  leaf blowers have become more readily available .   T he Township believes that the passage of  this ordinance will more clearly reflect the needs and values of the West Orange community.

pages.text: 99-25  March 25, 2025  RESOLUTION  TO AUTHORIZE THE   REPAIR OF   STREET   SWEEPER   THROUGH   THE   BERGEN  COUNTY   OF   NJ COOPERATIVE   FOR   THE   D E PARTMENT OF PUBLIC WORKS  WHEREAS ,   the   Department   of   Public   Works   (DPW)   is   looking   to   repair   one   of   its   two  (2)   st r eet   sweepers   the   township   owns .   The   street   sweeper ’ s   clean   200   lane   miles   of   roadway  within our township ; and  WHEREAS ,   the   T ownship   is   required   to   keeps   the   roadways   clean   to   be   in   compliance  with   the   Municipal   Separate   Storm   Sewers   (MS4)   Tier   A   Permit   that   is   issued   and   regulated   by  the   New   Jersey   Department   of   Environmental   Protection   ( NJDEP )   to   municipalities   to   prevent  solids and floatable materials from entering the MS4s ; and  WHEREAS,   as   a   local   government   unit   and   a   member   of   the   Bergen   C ounty  Cooperative   (NJ   Purchasing   Alliance) ,   can   purchase   and   repair   such   items ,   pursuant   to   the  provision   made   in   the   Local   Public   Contracts   Law   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(2) ,   without   public  advertising   for   such   goods   or   services ,   through   contracts   already   secured   by   the   United   States   of  America, the State of New   Jersey,   County or Municipality thereof; and  WHEREAS ,   the   vendor   W.E.   Timmerman,   located   3554   Route   22   West,   Whitehouse,  NJ   08888   i s   under   contract   with   large   scale   parts   &   repair ,   Bid   24-36   in   the   amount   of  $ 67,954.64.  WHEREAS,   the   Department   of   Public   Works   is   requesting   additional   allowance   of  $5000.00   for any unseen or extra repairs/ parts needed.  NOW,   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED ,   by   the   Municipal   Council   of   the   Township  of   West   Orange   who   hereby   authorize s   the   Purchasing   Agent,   on   behalf   of   the   Township ,   to   issue  and   execute   a   Purchase   Order   in   the   amount   not   to   exceed   $ 72,954.64   to   W.E   Timmerman   with  funds certified available by the Chief Financial Officer.  Karen   J.   Carn evale, Municipal Clerk   Joe Krakoviak , Council President  Adopted: March 25, 2025  I hereby certify funds available   from Account No.  John C. 100-25  March 25, 2 025  A RESOLUTION   APPROVING   A   CHANGE ORDER   FROM   CME ASSOCIATES   FOR  REMEDIATION   SERVICES   AT   25 LAKESIDE AVENUE   ( DPW BUILDING )  WHEREAS,   by   Resolution   350-23   on   October   10,   2023   attached   herein   as   Exhibit   “ A ” ,  the   Township   Council   approved   a   contract   for   Professional   Environmental   and   Licensed   Site  Remediation   Services   with   CME   Associates   (CME)   of   Monmouth   Junction,   N.J.   for   the   DPW  Building located at 25 Lakeside Avenue; and  WHEREAS,   a   recent   groundwater   analysis   at   the   site   identified   an   additional   Area   of  Concern (AOC) at the Salt Dome area of the property; and  WHEREAS,   the   additional   work   required   to   investigate   this   new   AOC   i s   not   included   in  the original contract; and  WHEREAS,   the   additional   work   required   to   investigate   the   AOC   requires   installation   of  two   new   monitoring   wells   and   conducting   additional   groundwater   testing   at   up   to   ten   locations  on site; and  NOW, BE IT HEREBY RESOLVED BY THE TOWNSHIP COUNCIL OF THE  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   that   the   Township   accepts   the   Change   Order   for   this  additional   work   from   CME   attached   herein   as   Exhibit   “ B , ”   not   to   exceed   $ 19 ,7 00.00   to  complete the scope of work ;  AND   FURTHER   RESOLVED,   tha t   a   copy   of   this   Resolution   shall   be   made   available   in  the Clerk ’ s office in accordance with applicable law. March 11, 2025  Ms. Zayibeth Carballo, PE,PLS,PP,CME,CFM,CFS  Director, Municipal Engineer  Department of Engineering  Township of West Orange  25 Lakeside Avenue, West Orange, NJ 07052  zcarballo@westorange.org  Mr. James Abbott  Business Administrator  66 Main Street  West Orange, NJ 07052  policechief@wopd.org  Re:   Professional Environmental and Licensed Site Remediation Services  Change Order for West Orange DPW at 25 Lakeside Avenue  West Orange RES 350-23 - Purchase Order # 182229  CME Project No. IWO-00380.13  Dear Ms. Carballo:  CME   Associates (CME ) is currently contracted with the Township for Professional Environmental and Licensed Site Remediation Services consulting work at the West Orange DPW located at 25 Lakeside Avenue (Site) . Recently, a contaminated Area of Concern (AOC) was identified at the Site (Salt Dome), the most recent groundwater analytical results confirmed a discharge at the Salt Dome as an additional impacted AOC which has to be investigated and remediated   in accordance with the NJDEP Regulations and Guidance.  Additional remedial investigation activities should be completed to delineate the exceedances of Metals associated with the Salt Dome discharge and identify the extent of this discharge.   The additional work required to investigate the new AOC is not included in the original proposal.  A review of the groundwater sampling documents and results at the Site and the off-site adjacent property   Former Barton Press, it appeared that historical results for metals analysis showed exceedances for sodium at some of the Former Barton Press wells, located both in the upgradient and downgradient of the AOC source area (Salt Dome).  The   investigation   at   the   off-site   adjacent   property   (Former   Barton   Press)   is   required   to   complete   the   groundwater delineation/groundwater remedial investigation at the DPW Site.  The additional work required to investigate the new AOC includes installation of two additional monitoring wells and conducting groundwater sampling at selected wells at the   Former Barton Press property and the DPW property for the analysis of Metals at the same time for comparison. The following   scope of work is required to comply with the NJDEP Technical Requirements for Site Remediation at the Site and necessary to continue to advance the project towards closure.

Ms. Zayibeth Carballo, PE,PLS,PP,CME,CFM,CFS Director, Municipal Engineer   March 11, 2025 Re:   Department of Public Works at 25 Lakeside Avenue – Change Order   Page   2  P:\I00 CME7\West Orange\DPW 25 Lakeside Ave\Budget - Proposal- DPW FILE\March 2023 RI Proposal\Change Order - Additional wells and sampling - West Orange DPW - 3-11-2025.docx  Additional two monitoring wells will be installed, and a groundwater sampling at selected 10 monitoring wells will be conducted and analyzed for Metals to determine the extent of the AOC discharge impacts.  CME will retain a licensed driller to install the monitoring wells with 2-inch diameter PVC; the wells will be constructed to a depth of approximately (20) feet below ground surface (bgs).   The depths and locations of the monitoring wells will be determined based on the findings of the investigations conducted to date to delineate the extent of contaminants identified during the initial sampling event. The wells will be completed with a flush-mount cover. Drill cuttings will be placed in drums and properly disposed off-site at a licensed facility, and development water will be drummed for off-site disposal or properly treated prior to on- site discharge. A professional licensed surveyor will survey the monitoring wells. Well logs and Form A and Form B will be prepared to properly document the well construction.  One round of groundwater samples will be collected from ten (10) selected monitoring wells to delineate the impacted groundwater and provide a baseline dataset for further remedial investigation, if needed. Sampling will be performed in accordance with USEPA Low Flow Sampling Procedures (March 1998). Water levels will be measured in all monitoring wells to confirm the groundwater flow direction. Water quality parameters will be directly measured in the field including conductivity, pH, temperature, redox potential, salinity, and dissolved oxygen (DO). All groundwater samples will be submitted for laboratory analysis for TAL Metals in accordance with the TRSR and NJDEP guidance documents.  Our estimated cost to complete the additional required services is $19,700.  Task   Description   Cost  1   Monitoring Wells Installation   $ 10,200  2   Groundwater Sampling   $ 9,500  We appreciate your consideration of the above and would be happy to answer any questions regarding this request.  Respectfully submitted,  CME Associates  _________________________________  Behram Turan, P.E., LSRP  Senior Vice President  cc: Michael McClelland, P.E., P.P. - CME Associates
