RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING FIREWORKS AT ESSEX COUNTY COUNTRY CLUB - 2025

pages.text: 108-25  April 8, 2025  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   an   application   has   been   submitted   by   the   Essex   County   Country   Club ,  which   wishes   to   sponsor   its   annual   Fourth   of   July   fireworks   exhibition   at   the   Essex   County  Country Club grounds   on July   3 , 20 2 5   (Rain Date: July   6 , 20 2 5 ); and  WHEREAS,   all   of   the   necessary   documentation   has   been   submitted   to   the   Township  Clerk,   on   an   Application   for   Permission   to   Conduct   Fireworks   Display   (the   “ Application ” ),   as  required by State and local law; and  WHEREAS,   the   Application   indicates   that   Garden   State   Fi reworks,   Inc.,   383   Carlton  Road, Millington, New Jersey, 07946, will provide and supervise the fireworks display; and  WHEREAS,   the   Application   has   been   reviewed   and   approved   by   the   Township   Fire  Official,   and   confirms   that   a   Certificate   of   Insurance   naming   the   Township   as   an   additional  insured has been provided .  NOW   THEREFORE   BE   IT   RESOLVED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE   that   the   Essex   County   Country   Club   be   and   is   hereby  granted   permission   to   conduct   a   firework   display   on   July   3 ,   2 0 25   (Rain   Date:   July   6 ,   20 25 ) ;  subject to the directives of the Fire Official, or his designees.  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Township Clerk   Council President  Adopted:   April 8, 2025

documents.title: AUTHORIZING EXECUTION OF PROFESSIONAL SERVICES AGREEMENT WITH BLAU AND BLAU FOR COMMERCIAL TAX APPEALS - 2025

pages.text: 111-25  April 8, 2025  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   Blau   and   Blau   has   provided   professional   services   to   the   Township   of   West  Orange in connection with commercial real estate tax appeals since September 2012; and  WHEREAS,   Blau   and   Blau   provided   responses   to   the   Township ’ s   Request   for  Qualifications pursuant to a fair and open process for the year   2025 ; and  WHEREAS,   Blau   &   Blau   has   agreed   to   provide   professional   services   for   the   calendar  year   2025 ,   pursuant   to   the   terms   and   conditions   set   forth   in   the   agreement   annexed   hereto   as  Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and  WHEREAS ,   the   Law   Department   recommends   retention   of   Blau   &   Blau   as   special  counsel   to   the   Township   for   the   purpose   of   representing   the   Township   in   commercial   real   estate  tax appeals; and  WHEREAS ,   pursuant   to   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   40A:11-5(1)(a)(i),   the   proposed  professional   services   contract   is   subject   to   an   exception   to   the   standard   bidding   procedures   set  forth in   the Local Public Contracts   Law;  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Mayor   be   and   is   hereby   authorized   to   execute   an   agreement,   in   the   form  annexed   hereto,   to   retain   Blau   and   Blau   for   the   provision   of   commercial   real   estate   tax   appeal   in  accordance   with   Exhibit   “ A ”   and   the   Municipal   Clerk   shall   be   and   hereby   is   authorized   to   attest  to the Mayor ’ s signature; and it is further  RESOLVED   that   notice   of   this   award   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.

4843-6064-6118, v. 1  Karen J. Carnevale , R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: April 8, 2025  I hereby certify funds are available from   Account No. :  John Ditinyak ,   Chief Financial Officer

111-25  AGREEMENT FOR COMMERCIAL REAL ESTATE TAX APPEALS  THIS   AGREEMENT   made   and   entered   into   on   this   8 th   day   of   April ,   2025   by   and  between   the   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   a   municipal   corporation   of   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   located   at   Town   Hall,   66   Main   Street,   West   Orange,   County   of   Essex   and   State   of   New  Jersey,   hereinafter   called   the   “ TOWNSHIP , ”   party   of   the   first   part,   and   BLAU   &   BLAU ,  Attorneys   at   Law   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   located   at   223   Mountain   Avenue,   Springfield,   New  Jersey 07081, hereinafter referred to as   “ COUNSEL , ”   party of the second part.  WITNESSETH THAT:  WHEREAS,   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Township   has   determined   that   it   is   in   the   best  interests   of   the   Township   to   retain   the   services   of   Special   Counsel   to   represent   the   Township   in  connection with commercial real estate tax appeals on a flat fee basis;  NOW,   THEREFORE,   in   consideration   of   the   mutual   promises,   terms   and   conditions  hereinafter set forth, the parties hereto agree as follows:  1.   The   Township   hereby   retains   the   services   of   Blau   &   Blau,   as   Special   Counsel   to  assist   the   Township   in   connection   with   commercial   real   estate   tax   appeals   for   the   period   January  1,   2025   through   December   31,   2025   at   a   flat   rate   of   $ 6 ,000   per   month   for   the   first   forty   (40)  cases   filed   in   2025   and   each   additional   case   at   a   rate   of   $1,500   per   case.   There   will   be   an   hourly  rate of $150 per hour for trials, including pre-trial preparation and post-trial work.  2.   COUNSEL   will   not   seek   re imbursement   for   its   routine   costs   and   ex pense s   such   as  postage, copying, mil e age, meals, parking, or delivery of documents.  3.   COUNSEL   may   se ek   reimbursement   for   extraordin ary   expenses   such   as   the   cost  of   deposing   adverse   witnesses,   transcripts   or   hiring   experts.   Counsel   will   not   incur   any   such  expenses without the approval of the Township Attorney.

2  4812-2524-8993, v. 1  4.   COUNSEL ’ S   responsibilities   shall   include   all   pre-trial   preparation,   discovery   and  trial,   to   the   extent   necessary,   for   all   commercial   real   estate   tax   appeals   pending   or   commenced   in  the   Tax   Court   of   New   Jersey   against   the   Township.   Counsel ’ s   responsibilities   do   not   include  preparation or appearance for matters pending or commenced at the County Board of Taxation.  5.   COUNSEL   shall   coordinate   all   efforts   with   the   Tax   Ass e ssor   and   keep   the   Tax  Ass e ssor and Township Attorney fully apprised concerning all matters.  6.   Prosecution of Appeals fo r the Township ( Reverse   Appeals).  COUNSEL   shall   have   the   right   to   file   tax   appeals   for   the   Township   o n   a   contingent   fee  basis   of   one- third   of   the   money   obtain ed   for   the   Town ship   from   cases   filed   dur ing   the   term   of   the  contract.   COUNSEL   will   be   responsible   for   all   costs   associated   with   filing   the   affirmative  appeals including but not limited to:  A) Filing fees;  B) Appraisal fees:  C) Copying, postage, delivery services, preparation of trial exhibits , etc .  It   is   contemplated   that   COUNSEL   will   recommend   appeals   on   properties   and   will   not   be  obligated   to   file   an   appeal   unless   it   believes   the   property   is   substantially   under- assessed.   It   is  contemplated   that   COUNSEL   will   recommend   settlement   of   the   Affirmative   Appeals   from   time  to   time   as   COUNSEL   may   deem   appropriate.   In   the   event   that   the   Township   refuses   to   accept  COUNSEL ’ s   recommendation   to   settle   a   matter,   the   Township   shall   be   responsible   for   payment  of   the   contingent   fee   based   upon   the   recommendation   and   r eimbursement   of   COUNSEL ’ s   actual  out of pocket expenses related to that Affirmative Appeal.  7.   COUNSEL   recognizes   that   any   and   all   approval   for   engagement   of   services   must  come   from   the   Governing   Body   of   the   Township,   and   that   no   services,   work   or   any   efforts

3  4812-2524-8993, v. 1  relative   to   the   contract   shall   be   commenced   until   the   Governing   Body   passes   a   duly   authorized  Resolution accompanied by a Certificate of Funds.  8.   COUNSEL   is   hereby   placed   on   notice   that   the   Billing   Guidelines   of   the   Township  shall   apply   to   this   agreement   for   professional   services.   COUNSEL   is   further   noticed   that   no  Department   Head,   individual   member   of   the   Governing   Body,   or   any   agent,   servant   or   employee  of   the   Township   possesses   any   lawful   authority   to:   (a)   engage   the   rendition   of   services   or   the  performance   of   work;   (b)   authorize   the   continuation   of   services   or   work   beyond   the   amount  specifically   approved   in   the   Resolution   and   Certificate   of   Fund s;   or   to   (c)   represent   that   future  funds   will   be   available   as   compensation   for   current   services.   Any   such   acts   shall   be   deemed  ultra   vires   and   beyond   the   scope   of   any   authority   that   individual   may   possess.   No   bills,  statement   or   vouchers   for   any   amount   exceeding   that   originally   approved   will   be   honored   or   paid  by the Township, irrespective of whether such services were actually performed.  9.   During   the   term   of   this   agreement,   COUNSEL   shall   maintain   professional  liability   malpractice   insurance   coverage   with   an   insurance   company   licensed   and   authorized   to  do   business   in   the   State   of   New   Jersey   with   coverage   not   less   than   $1,000,000.   COUNSEL   shall  provide   the   Township   Attorney   and   Municipal   Clerk   with   proof   of   a   valid   certificate   of   insurance  listing   the   TOWNSHIP   as   a   certificate   holder.   To   the   extent   that   this   insurance   coverage   is  scheduled   to   lapse   at   any   time   before   the   end   of   the   contract,   COUNSEL   shall   provide   proof   of  renewal   or   new   insurance   coverage   no   later   than   thirty   (30)   days   before   the   termination   of   the  current coverage.  10.   The   provisions   of   this   agreement   are   subject   to   the   limitations   of   provisions   of   the  New   Jersey   Tort   Claim   Act,   N.J.S.A.   59:2-1   et   seq. ,   and   the   New   Jersey   Contractual   Liability  Act,   N.J.S.A.   59:13-1   et seq.

4  4812-2524-8993, v. 1  11.   This   agreement   shall   be   governed   by   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   without  regard   to   principles   of   conflict   of   laws.   Al l   contract   claims   under   this   agreement   shall   be   subject  to   and   governed   by   the   provisions   of   the   New   Jersey   Contractual   Liability   Act   ( N.J.S.A.   59:13-1  et seq.  IN   WITNESS   WHEREOF,   the   parties   have   set   their   hands   and   seals   the   day   and   year  first above written.  ATTEST:  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C  TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  By:  SUSAN McCARTNEY , MAYOR  WITNESS:  BLAU & BLAU  By:  CHARLES E. BLAU, ESQ.  For the Firm

documents.title: RESOLUTION AUTHORIZING ONLINE AUCTION - FILE CABINETS 2025

pages.text: 112- 25  April 8, 2025  Resolution  Authorizing Disposal of Surplus Property  WHEREAS , the Township of West Orange is the owner of certain surplus property which is no  longer needed for public use; and  WHEREAS , the Township is desirous of selling said surplus property in an   “ As is ”   condition  without express or implied warranties.  NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED   by the West Orange Council of the Township of  West Orange:  (1) The sale of the surplus property shall be conducted through Municibid   pursuant to State  Contract 19-GNSV1-00696 in accordance with the terms and conditions of the State Contract.  The terms and conditions of the agreement entered into with Municibid is available online at  www.municibids.com and available on the Township Website.  (2) The sale will be conducted online and the address of the auction site is   www.municibid.com  at a date and time to be determined.  (3) There is no listing fee charged to the Township.  (4) The sale will be conducted pursuant to N.J.S.A 40A:11-36 and Local Finance Notice 2019-  15.  (5) A list of the surplus property is attached as   “ Exhibit A ”   hereto.  (6) The surplus property as identified shall be sold in an   “ as-is ”   condition without express or  implied warranties with the successful bidder required to execute a Hold Harmless and  Indemnification Agreement concerning use of said surplus property.  (7) The Township reserves the right to accept or reject any bid submitted.  Karen J.   Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted : April 8, 2025

pages.text: 113-25  April 8, 2025  RESOLUTION  WHEREAS,   the   Township   of   West   Orange   (the   “ Township ” )   seeking   services   of   a  cancer   screening   for   the   West   Orange   Fire   Department   to   provide   consulting   and   screening  services for the Township of West Orange   Fire Department   ( “ Cancer Screening ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   o n   November   13,   2024 ,   the   Township   advertised   a   Request   for  Qualifications   (the   “ RF Q ” )   for   the   award   of   an   Agreement   for   Cancer   Screening   for   the   West  Orange Fire Department   (the   “ Contract ” ) ; and  WHEREAS ,   National   Diagnostic   Service   ( “ NDS ” )   submitted   a   Proposal   seeking   award  of   the   Contract   for   Cancer   Screening   for   the   West   Orange   Fire   Department   in   response   to   the  RFQ ; and  WHEREAS,   NDS   included   in   their   Proposal   for   Professional   Services   a   proposed   fee  schedule as follows:  Non-State Health Benefits Participants  Physical Exam, Skin and Oral Cancer Screening:   $275.00  Comprehensive Blood Panel (CBC, CMP, Lipids):   $125.00  PSA and TSH Blood Analysis:   $75.00  Urine Bladder Cancer Screen: $15.00  Fecal Occult Colon Cancer Screen: $25.00  CT Low Dose Lung Cancer Screen: $250.00  Ultrasound Cancer Screening: $400.00  a.   Thyroid, Liver, Kidney, Testicular/Pelvic  2 nd   Provider Result Review; TeleHealth: $80.00  a.   Blood analysis and Radiology review  WHEREAS,   pursuant   to   N.J.S.A.   52:14-17.29ii(a)   the   Township   shall   be   reimbursed   for  such costs from the State; and  WHEREAS ,   the Contract will   be for the calendar year   2025 .  NOW,   BE   IT   HEREBY   RESOLVED,   by   the   Township   Council   of   the   Township   of  West   Orange   that   the   Township   authorizes   the   award   and   execution   of   the   Contract   with

National Diagnostic Services , annexed hereto as Exhibit   “ A ; ”   and be it further  RESOLVED   that   a   copy   of   this   resolution   shall   be   available   in   the   Clerk ’ s   office   in  accordance with applicable law.  Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.   Joe Krakoviak  Municipal Clerk   Council President  Adopted: April 8, 2025  I hereby certify funds are available from Account No.:   01-2010-00-7101-200  John Ditinyak, Chief Financial Officer

113-25  SERVICES AGREEMENT BETWEEN   NATIONAL DIAGNOSTIC  SERVICES   AND THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  This   service   agreement   is   between   the   Township   of   West   Orange   ( “ Township ” )   and  National   Diagnostic   Services   ( “ NDS ” )   28700   Cabot   Drive,   Suite   500,   Novi,   MI   48377,  Attention: Michael Ketslakh, President .  SECTION ONE: SCOPE OF SERVICES  For   the   2025   calendar   year ,   NDS   hereby   agrees   to   serve   as   a   consultant   for   cancer   screening  services   to   the   West   Orange   Fire   Department .   The   scope   of   services   provided   by   NDS   is   as  follows:  2 Phases  a.   Phase 1   - On site or in office visit  Physical & Blood draw- Provider review of complete health history, Oral Cancer  Screening & Full Skin Cancer Screening.  Cancer Screening Ultrasound  i.   Script provided for Low   Dose   Lung CT Screening   &   appt  scheduled at an offsite location convenient to member  ii.   Telehealth appt 1 week out from onsite or in office visit  appt  Testing should include, at a minimum, testing and screenings for the following  types of cancer in accordance with N.J.S.A. 52:14- 17.29ii(a)(1)-(11):  1. Colon  2. Lung  3. Bladder  4. Oral  5. Thyroid  6.   Sk in  7. Blood  8. Breast  9. Cervical  1 o. Testicular  11 . Prostate  Phase 2   - 20-minute telehealth results review-Provider consults on patient results  (Ultrasound & comprehensive Blood Work review). 11 member completed the CT  screening and results are ready the consult will include those as well.

2  i.   All members have access to their complete medical record &  results in a medical system provided by you. Vendor to provide  login instructions for   accessing those results to ease follow up.  ii.   Provider follow up ensures all members understand their results  and if any follow up is required they are directed appropriately to  their primary care provider.  Additional Services to be included:  a. provides all mar k eting & scheduling materials.  i.   Registration information- Demographics of members (for lab  requisitions), eligibility (active members only and have at least 3  years as an active-duty firefighter).  SECTION   TWO :   COMPENSATION  The   Township   agrees   to   p ay   NDS   per   its   fee   schedule   attached   hereto .   Pursuant   to  N.J.S.A.   52:14-17.29ii(a) the Township shall be reimbu r sed for such costs from the State.  SECTION THREE: BILLING PROCEDURES  NDS   shall   submit   the   proper   vouchers   or   invoices   to   the   Township   in   accordance   with  applicable   law   and   regulations.   Failure   to   submit   invoices   within   30   days   of   the   conclusion   of   a  quarter shall void the payment to   NDS   for that quarter.  SECTION FOUR: STATUS OF CONSULTANT  It   is   hereby   agreed   that   NDS   is   an   independent   contract or   and   is   not   considered   an  employee   of   the   Town ship   of   West   Orange   during   the   performance   of   its   duties   as   outlined   in  Section   One.   The   Town ship   of   West   Orange   agree   to   defend   the   consultant   in   the   event   a   claim  or   lawsuit   is   bought   against   the   Town ship   for   any   actions   alleging   negligence   with   respect   to   the  administration   of   the   insurance   programs.   It   is   also   understood   that   the   Town ship   of   West  Orange   will   cooperate   fully   with   the   consultant   in   the   performance   of   his   duties   as   outlined   in  Section One.  S ECTION FIVE: NON ASSIGNMENT  Consultant   is   prohibited   from   assigning   this   agreement   to   any   other   parties   without   the  written authorization from the Town ship .  SECTION SIX: TERM, TERMINATION and JURISTICTION  The   term   of   this   agreement   is   for   the   2025   calendar   year .   It   is   understood   that   the  continuation   of   this   contract   is   pending   the   availability   of   funds   in   subsequent   fiscal   years   and   if  the   funds   are   not   available   in   subsequent   fiscal   years   then   this   agreement   shall   be   canceled.   The

3  terms   of   this   agreem ent   are   subject   to   the   laws   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey   and   any   disputes  between   the   parties   shall   be   heard   in   the   applicable   court   of   law.   This   agreement   may   be  cancelled with thirty (30) days ’   written notice by either party.  * Township   and   NDS   agree   that   West   Orange   Mayor   Susan   McCartney   has   not   been   involved   in  any   aspect   of   this   Agreement,   including,   but   not   limited   to,   solicitation,   negotiation,  consideration or award of this Agreement . *  In   witness   whereof,   the   signatures   below   verify   the   acceptance   of   the   terms   of   this   agreement   by  the parties involved. Signed on this   _____   day in the month of   April   in the year   2025 .  ___________________________________   ___________________________________  MICHAEL KETSLAKH, PRESIDENT   WITNESS  ___________________________________   ___________________________________  JAMES P. ABBOTT ,   ATTEST: Karen J. Carnevale, R.M.C.  BUSINESS ADMINISTRATOR

2904-25  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This   Ordinance   eliminates   the   8AM-10AM   One   Hour   Par king   Zone s   on   Colony   Drive   East,   Pitney   Street,  and   Swayze   Street   and   create s   new   8AM-10AM   No   Parking   Zone s   there   and   at   Elliott   Place   to   discourag e  all - day   parking   by   Seton   Hall   Prep   students ,   NYC   commuters ,   and   Parents/Teachers   at   Kelly   Elementary  School   during   school   opening   hours,   and   allow   parking   at   other   times   for   the   residents.   This   change   was  recommended by the   Traffic Division of WOPD , and in respons e to local residents ’   requests.

1  2907-25  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING   AND SUPPLEMENTING   CHAPTER   2,   SECTION   ___  OF THE REVISED GENERAL ORDINANCES OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST  ORANGE   TO ESTABLISH A CITIZENS BUDGET ADVISORY BOARD  BE   IT   ORDAINED   BY   THE   MUNICIPAL   COUNCIL   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   OF  WEST   ORANGE,   NEW   JERSEY   that   Chapter   2   of   the   Revised   General   Ordinances   of   the  Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The   purpose   of   this   Ordinance   is   to   establish   a   Citizens   Budget   Advisory   Board   via  Ordinance .  § 2-____: Definitions  Ci tizens   Budget   Advisory   Board:   An   advisory   board   made   up   of   members   of   the   public  appointed   by   both   the   Township   Council   and   the   Mayor   charged   with   reviewing   the   Township ’ s  Budget,   its   services,   programs   and   expenditures,   compiling   the   Board ’ s   collective   analysis,   input  and   suggestions   and   presenting   them   to   the   Township   Council   by   regular   reports   and  presentations, as appropriate .  § 2-____: Establishment of a Citizens Advisory Board  A.   The   Citizens   Budget   Advisory   Board   shall   be   comprised   of:   (i)   one   member  ap pointed   by   the   Mayor;   (ii)   o ne   member   appointed   by   each   of   the   five   members   of  the   Township   Council;   and   (iii)   one   consensus   candidate   chosen   by   the   majority   of  the Council.  B.   Candidates   for   the   board   shall   be   Township   residents,   work   in   the   Township,  employed   by   a   company   based   in   the   Township,   or   own   or   lease   property   in   the  Township.   The   chair   shall   be   selected   by   a   majority   vote   of   the   members,   as   promptly  as   practicable   once   appointments   are   made.   The   term   of   each   Board   member   will   be  one   calendar   year ,   starting   on   January   1   of   the   year   that   the   members   serve,   and  members   may   be   reappointed   in   subsequent   years.   Appointments   shall   be   made  before   January   1   of   the   year   that   members   serve.   In   the   case   of   any   newly   elected  Councilmember,   his/her   appointment,   as   well   as   the   appointment   of   the   Council  consensus, should be made as soon as practicable once he/she takes the oath of office.  C.   A   Board   member   can   resign   by   sending   a   letter   or   email   to   the   Municipal   Clerk   which  shall   take   effect   immediately.   Any   Board   member   may   be   removed   with   or   without  cause, at any time, by the person appointing the Board member.  D.   Board   members   are   required   to   attend   all   scheduled   Board   meetings   and   strongly  encouraged   to   attend   all   Council   meetings   that   focus   on   the   budget,   including   review  of   any   video   or   audio   recordings   of   such   meetings.   Failure   to   attend   three   Board

2  meetings   may   be   grounds   for   removal   from   the   Board   by   a   written   motion   to   dismiss  that   can   be   offered   by   any   Board   member.   The   Board   member   at   issue   may   request   a  p ublic   hearing   of   the   cause(s)   and   present   a   defense.   That   hearing   shall   be   followed  by   deliberation   in   open   session.   A   majority   vote   decides   the   issue,   with   a   tie   vote  allowing the Board member to continue to serve.  E.   The Board shall:  a.   Make   all   requests   for   information   or   materials   following   a   majority   vote   of   the  members present.  b.   Have   access   to   all   financial   and   budget   documents   that   it   believes   are   needed  to   advise   the   Council   that   are   not   otherwise   subject   to   privacy   or   other   non-  disclosure   limitations .   The   Board   shall   make   its   request   in   writing   to   the  Council,   which   will   review   the   request   and   forward   it   to   the   Business  Administrator or his/her designee, and the Chief Financial Officer.  c.   Request   in   writing   through   the   Council   to   the   Business   Administrator   or  his/her   designee,   and   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   to   meet   with   the   Chief  Financial   Officer   or   others   as   appropriate   for   presentations   and   discussions   on  these financial issues outside of a regular or special Council meeting.  d.   Take   m inutes   of   meeti ngs   and   submit   approved   Minutes   to   the   Council,  Business   Administrator   and   Chief   Financial   Officer.   The   Board   will   also  provide   the   m inutes   to   the   Municipal   Clerk   for   public   availability   in   the  Clerk ’ s office and for posting to the Township website .  e.   Submit   written   reports   to   the   Council,   Business   Administrator   and   Chief  Financial   Officer   on   a   regular   basis   of   the   Board ’ s   findings,   questions   and  recommendations.  f.   Appear   as   requested   by   the   Town   Council   at   Council   meetings   to   present  and/or   engage   in   discus sions   with   Councilmembers   and   Administration  personnel, as appropriate.  g.   Meet   at   least   once   a   month   in   the   year   that   members   serve,   because   Budget  issues   are   a   year-round   responsibility,   including   year-end   account  reconciliations   and   pre-planning   for   the   following   calendar   year.   Meetings  may occur more frequently depending on the majority vote of members.

3  h.   Communicate   as   appropriate   with   the   Councilmember   or   Mayor   who  appointed   them   on   budget   matters.   Members   can   also   communicate   through  the   Board   Chair   or   his/her   designee   with   county   or   state   agencies   as  appropriate,   as   well   as   other   municipalities   and   other   entities   that   may   perform  similar   advisory   duties   in   other   municipalities.   When   communicating,   the  Chair   or   designee   shall   clearly   describe   his   or   her   role   as   a   member   of   the  West   Orange   Citizens   Budget   Advisory   Board,   a   volunteer   body   created   by  Ordinance   advising   the   Township   Council   to   develop   the   annual   budget   and  related financial areas.  I I .   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any   Ordinances   of   the   Township   which   are   in   conflict   with   this   Ordinance   are   hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  I II .   SEVERABILITY  If   any   part   of   this   Ordinance   shall   be   deemed   invalid,   such   determination   shall   only   apply  to   those   specific   section(s),   paragraph(s),   subparagraph(s),   clause(s)   or   provision(s)   and   those  parts   shall   be   severed .   T h e   invalidity   thereof   shall   not   a ffect   the   remaining   parts   of   this  Ordinance   which shall be deemed valid and effective.  IX .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This   Ordinance   shall   take   effect   u pon   final   passage   and   publication   in   accordance   with  the law.  SUSAN McCARTNEY, MAYOR  JOE KRAKOVIAK  COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J. CARNEVALE, R.M.C.,  MUNICIPAL CLERK  INTRODUCED:   March 25, 2025  ADOPTED:   April 8, 2025

4  2 907-25  Legislative History  This   Ordinance   will   establish   the   creation   of   a   Citizens   Board   Advisory   Board   (the  “ Board ” )   by   Ordinance.   In   years   past,   the   Township   previously   had   the   Board   by   Resolution.  The purpose of this Ordinance is to codify the rules and regulations of the Board via Ordinance.

2 908 - 2 5  AN ORDINANCE AMENDING CHAPTER   2 , S E CTION   9. 4   GENERAL ORDINANCES  OF THE TOWNSHIP OF WEST ORANGE  ( SPECIAL COUNSEL )  BE IT ORDAINED BY THE MUNICIPAL COUNCIL OF THE TOWNSHIP OF  WEST ORANGE,   NEW JERSEY   that Chapter   2, Section s   9. 4   o f   the Revised General  Ordinances of the Township of West Orange be and   are   hereby amended as follows:  I.   PURPOSE  The purpose of this ordinance is   to   modify the   provisions of the Township of West  Orange   Municipal   Code   governing   the   Township’s   retention   of   Special   Counsel .   This  ordinance: (i) requires appointment of Special Counsel by advice and consent of the Township  Council; and (ii) require that the bills for Special Counsel shall be subject to approval from the  Township Council.  I I .   CHAPTER   2 SECTION S   9.4   SHALL BE AND HEREBY IS AMENDED AND  SUPPLEMENTED   TO INCORPORATE THE FOLLOWING:  2 - 9. 4   Special Counsel .  Subject to   the advise   and consent of the   Township Council , the Township Attorney may appoint  such Special Counsel as may be required or advisable to handle matters in which a conflict of  interest precludes representation by the Township Attorney or Assistant Township Attorney, or  which due to its complexity, special nature, time demands or constraints, or for any good reason  would benefit from the special experience and/or training of the Special Counsel to be hired.  Such Special Counsel need not serve full time and would be expected to serve for the duration of  the matter for which he or she was appointed, subject to annual reappointment with the manner  and amount of compensation to be approved by the   Township   Coun cil . Special Counsel would  be subject to the supervision of the Township Attorney   except for matters   involving a conflict of  interest precluding representation by the Township Attorney wherein   Special Counsel would be  subject to the supervision of the   Assistant Township Attorney   or designated conflict counsel .   T he  bills of all Special Counsel   shall be subject to approval by the   Township Council. The Township  Council must receive written notification from the Administration detailing the reasons for  engaging Special Counsel,   including the name of the law firm to be engaged, the rate, and a “not  to exceed” clause.  III.   REPEAL OF CONFLICTING ORDINANCES  Any Ordinances of the Township which are in conflict with   this Ordinance are hereby  repealed to the extent of such conflict.  IV .   SEVERABILITY  If any part of this Ordinance shall be deemed invalid, such parts shall be severed and th e  invalidity thereof shall not a ffect the remaining parts of this   Ordinance.

2  V .   EFFECTIVE DATE  This Ordinance shall take effect upon final passage and publication in accordance with  the law.  SUSAN MCCARTNEY , MAYOR  JOSEPH KRAKOVIAK ,  COUNCIL PRESIDENT  KAREN J.   CARNEVALE, R.M.C.  MUNICIPAL CLERK  INTRODUCED:   March 25, 2025  ADOPTED:   April 8, 2025

3  2908 - 25  Legislative History  The purpose of this ordinance is   to modify the provisions of the Township of West  Orange   Municipal   Code   governing   the   Township’s   retention   of   Special   Counsel.   This  ordinance: (i) requires appointment of Special Counsel by advice and consent of the Township  Council; and (ii) require that the bills for Special Counsel shall be subject to approval from the  Township Council .

2909 - 25  LEGISLATIVE HISTORY  This Ordinance is in response to requests from handicapped residents who reside at 26 Babcock  Place, 268 Watchung Avenue, and 39 Washington Street.  By adopting this Ordinance, the Township will establish a handicapped parking stall adjacent to  each of these addresses. In the case of 39 Washington Street, the stall will be placed on the side of the  home on High Street, because the resident’s access if from that side. Such parking stalls are permissible  under the laws of New Jersey, and they are designated as Restricted Parking Zones. All other Parking  Ordinances under this chapter apply and supersede the use of these restricted spaces.  Additionally, two Handicapped Stalls located in Municipal Plaza nearest the Police Building will  get limits of 1 hour parking for the convenience of residents that have business there. Two other stalls  will be designated Handicapped for use of employees or others that need to park all day.

The purpose of this capital ordinance is to appropriate   $ 1,000,000   received  or expected to be received from a grant provided by the   New Jersey Department of  Community   Affairs   –   Coronavirus   State   Fiscal   Recovery   Fund   to   pay   for   various  improvements   and upgrades to   (i)   the Oskar Schindler Performing Arts Center   including,  but not limited to,   the installation of a sound system for the amphitheater,   a shed,   the  installation of ADA   accessible seating   ,   and the installation of safety lighting   on the  building,   stage flooring ,   and   security cameras   (ii) Crystal Lake, including, but not limited to,  the   construction of   two floating docks   .   S aid   improvement s   or purpose s   shall also  include, as applicable, all work, materials, equipment, engineering, design, consulting  work, preparation of plans and specifications, permits, bid documents, conducting and  preparation   of   reports   and   studies,   environmental   investigations   and   remediation,  equipment rental, labor and appurtenances necessary therefore or incidental thereto.  This capital ordinance will be considered for final adoption by the   Township  Council of the   Township   after public hearing thereon, at a meeting of the governing body  taking place at   the   Township   Municipal Building,   66 Main Street ,   West Orange,   New  Jersey ,   to be held on   April   8 ,   2025   at 7: 00   p.m.   A copy of this ordinance ma y be  obtained without cost from   the Office of the   Township   Clerk,   66 Main Street, West Orange ,  New Jersey , during regular business hours .  ___________________________________  KAREN J. A   Handicapped   Parking   S pace   on   the   Street   will   be   established   opposite   125   Elm   Street,   because  there   is   no   opportunity   to   park   legally   in   front   of   or   on   the   side   of   the   residence   due   to   other   parking  regulations. SECTION 4.   Except   for   the   Grants,   i n   the   event   the   United   States   of  America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of   Essex   make   a   contribut ion   or   grant  in   aid   to   the   Township   for   the   improvements   and   purposes   authorized   hereby   and   the   same  shall   be   received   by   the   Township   prior   to   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes   authorized   in  Section   2   hereof,   then   the   amount   of   such   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   shall   be   reduced   by  the   amount   so   received   from   the   United   States   of   America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or  the   County   of   Essex.   Except   for   the   Grants,   i n   the   event   that   any   amount   so   contributed   or  granted   by   the   United   States   of   America,   the   St ate   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of  Essex   shall   be   received   by   the   Township   after   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes  authorized   in   Section   2   hereof,   then   such   funds   shall   be   applied   to   the   payment   of   the  bonds   or   notes   so   issued   and   shall   be   used   for   no   other   purpose.   This   Section   4   shall   not  apply,   however,   with   respect   to   any   contribution   or   grant   in   aid   received   by   the   Township   as  a   result   of   using   funds   from   this   bond   ordinance   as   “ matching   local   funds ”   to   receive   such  contribution or grant in aid.  SECTION 5.   All   bond   anticipation   notes   issued   hereunder   shall   mature   at  such   times   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   (the   “ Chief  Financial   Officer ” ),   provided   that   no   note   shall   mature   later   than   one   (1)   year   from   its   date.

5  #95204340.1 168182.012  The   notes   shall   bear   interest   at   such   rate   or   rates   and   be   in   such   form   as   may   be  determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   shall   determine   all  matters   in   connection   with   the   notes   issued   pursuant   to   this   bond   ordinance,   a nd   the  signature   of   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   notes   shall   be   conclusive   evidence   as   to   all  such   determinations.   All   notes   issued   hereunder   may   be   renewed   from   time   to   time   in  accordance   w ith   the   provisions   of   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-8.1   of   the   Local   Bond   Law.   The   Chief  Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   sell   part   or   all   of   the   notes   from   time   to   time   at   a  public   or   private   sale   and   to   deliver   them   to   the   purchaser   thereof   upon   receipt   of   payment  of   the   purchase   price   and   accrued   interest   thereon   from   their   dates   to   the   date   of   delivery  thereof.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   the   governing   body   at  the   meeting   next   succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the   notes   pursuant   to  this   bond   ordinance   is   made.   Such   report   must   include   the   principal   amount,   the  description,   the   interest   rate,   the   maturity   schedule   of   the   notes   so   sold,   the   price   obtained  and the name of the purchaser.  SECTION 6.   The   Township   hereby   certifies   that   it   has   adopted   a   capital  budget   o r   a   temporary   capital   budget   of   the   Township,   as   applicable.   The   capital   budget   or  temporary   capital   budget,   as   applicable,   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   conform  with   the   provisions   of   this   bond   ordinance   to   the   extent   of   any   inconsistency   herewith.   To  the   extent   that   the   purposes   authorized   herein   are   inconsistent   with   the   adopted   capital   or  temporary   capital   budget,   a   revised   capital   or   temporary   capital   budget   has   been   filed   with  the Division of Local Government Services.  SECTION 7.   The   following   additional   matters   are   hereby   determined,  declared, recited and stated:  (a)   The   improvements   and   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this

6  #95204340.1 168182.012  bond   ordinance   are   not   current   expenses   and   are   improvements   and   purposes   which   the  Township   may   lawfully   undertake   as   general   improvements   and   no   part   of   the   cost   thereof  has been or shall be specially assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  (b)   The   period   of   usefulness   of   said   improvements   or   purposes,  computed   on   the   basis   of   the   respective   amounts   of   obligat ions   authorized   for   each   purpose  and   the   reasonable   life   thereof   in   accordance   with   the   limitations   set   forth   in   the   Local   Bond  Law, is   10   years.  (c)   The   supplemental   debt   statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond  Law   has   been   duly   made   and   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Clerk   of   the   Township   and   a   complete  executed   duplicate   thereof   has   been   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Director   of   the   Division   of  Local   Government   Services   in   the   Department   of   Community   Affairs   of   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   and   such   statement   shows   tha t   the   gross   debt   of   the   Township   as   defined   in   the  Local   Bond   Law   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   bonds   or   notes   provided   for   in   this  bond   ordinance   by   $ 6,714,669 .   The   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance   will   be  within all debt limitations prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  (d)   An   amount   not   exceeding   $ 780,000   for   items   of   expense   listed   i n  and   permitted   under   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-20   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   is   included   in   the   estimated  cost indicated herein for the purposes and improvements des cribed in Section 3 hereof.  SECTION 8.   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged  to   the   punctual   payment   of   the   principal   of   and   the   interest   on   the   obligations   authorized   by  this   bond   ordinance.   The   obligations   shall   be   direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township  and,   unless   paid   from   other   sources,   the   Township   shall   be   obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem  taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within   the   Township   for   the   payment   of   the   principal   of  the obligations and the interest thereon with out limitation as to rate or amount.

7  #95204340.1 168182.012  SECTION 9.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures   toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3  hereof   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   any   bonds   or   notes   authorized   by   this   bond  ordinance   with   the   proceeds   of   such   bonds   or   notes.   This   Section   9   is   intended   to   be   and  hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Township ’ s   official   intent   to   reimburse   any   expenditure   toward  the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Secti on   3   hereof   to   be   incurred   and  paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   bonds   or   notes   authorized   herein   in   accordance   with   Treasu ry  Regulation Section 1.150-2.  SECTION 10.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   prepare  and   to   update   from   time   to   time   as   necessary   a   financial   disclosure   document   to   be  distributed   in   connection   with   the   sale   of   the   obligations   of   the   Township   authorized   herein  and   to   execute   such   disclosure   document   on   behalf   of   the   Township.   The   Chief   Financial  Officer   is   further   author ized   to   enter   into   the   appropriate   undertaking   to   provide   secondary  market   disclosure   on   behalf   of   the   Township   pursuant   to   Rule   15c2-12   of   the   Securities   and  Exchange   Commission   (the   "Rule")   for   the   benefit   of   holders   and   beneficial   owners   of   the  obligations   of   the   Township,   which   are   authorized   herein,   and   to   amend   such   undertaking  from   time   to   time   in   connection   with   any   change   in   law,   or   interpretation   thereof,   provided  such   undertaking   is   and   continues   to   be,   in   the   opinion   of   a   nationally   recognized   b ond  counsel,   consistent   with   the   requirements   of   the   Rule.   In   the   event   that   the   Township   fails  to   comply   with   its   undertaking,   the   Township   shall   not   be   liable   for   any   monetary   damages,  and the remedy shall be limited to specific performance of the undertaking.  SECTION 11.   The   Township   covenants   to   maintain   the   exclusion   from  gross   income   under   Section   103(a)   of   the   Internal   Revenue   Code   of   1986,   as   amended,   of  the interest on all bonds and notes issued on a tax-exempt basis under this bond ordinance.

#95207920.1 168182.012  TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST ORANGE  COUNTY OF ESSEX, STATE OF NEW JERSEY  ORDINANCE NUMBER   291 6 -25  BOND   ORDINANCE   PROVIDING   FOR   BUILDING S   AND  GROUNDS   IMPROVEMENTS   ALONG   ROONEY   CIRCLE ,  BY   AND   IN   THE   T OWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE  COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW   JERSEY;  APPROPRIATING   $ 2, 170,000   THEREFOR   AND  AUTHORIZING   THE   ISSUANCE   OF   $ 2,066,840   BONDS   OR  NOTES   OF   THE   TOWNSHIP   TO   FINANCE   PART   OF   THE  COSTS THEREOF  BE   IT   ORDAINED   AND   ENACTED   BY   THE   TOWNSHIP   COUNCIL   OF  THE   TOWNSHIP   OF   WEST   ORANGE ,   IN   THE   COUNTY   OF   ESSEX ,   STATE   OF   NEW  JERSEY   (not   less   than   two-thirds   of   all   the   members   thereof   affirmativ ely   concurring),   AS  FOLLOWS:  SECTION   1 .   The   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this  bond   ordinance   are   hereby   authorized   as   general   improvements   or   purposes   to   be  undertaken   by   the   Township   of   West   Orange ,   in   the   County   of   Essex ,   State   of   New   Jersey  (the   “ Township ” ).   For   the   said   improvement s   or   purpose s   stated   in   Section   3,   there   is  hereby   appropriated   the   amount   of   $ 2,170,000 ,   said   amount   being   inclusive   of   a   down  payment   in   the   amount   of   $ 103,160   (the   “ Down   Payments ” )   for   said   improvements   or  purposes   as   required   b y   the   Lo cal   Bond   Law   of   the   State   of   New   Jersey ,   as   amended  and   supplemented   ( N.J.S.A.   40A:2-1   et   seq . )   (the   “ Local   Bond   Law ” ) ,   which   Down  Payment   is   now   available   from   the   Township ’ s   Capital   Improvement   Fund   by   virtue   of   a  provision   or   provisions   in   a   previously   adopted   budget   or   budgets   of   the   Township   for  down payment or capital improvement purposes .

2  #95207920.1 168182.012  SECTION   2 .   For   the   financing   of   said   improvements   or   purposes   described  in   Section   3   hereof   and   to   meet   the   part   of   said   $ 2,170,000   appropriation   not   provided   for  by   said   Down   Payment ,   negotiable   bonds   of   the   Township   are   hereby   authorized   to   be  issued   in   the   principal   amount   not   exceeding   $ 2,066,840   pursuant   to ,   and   within   the  limitations   prescribed   by ,   the   Local   Bond   Law.   In   anticipation   of   the   issuance   of   said  bonds   and   to   temporarily   finance   said   improvements   or   purposes,   negotiable   notes   of   the  Township   in   th e   principal   amount   not   exceeding   $ 2,066,840   are   hereby   authorized   to   be  issued pursuant to ,   and within the limitations prescribed by ,   said Local Bond Law.  SECTION   3 .   (a)   The   improvements   hereby   authorized   and   purposes   for  the   financing   of   which   said   obligations   are   to   be   issued   are   for   exterior   building   and  grounds   improvements   along   Rooney   Circle .   The   improvements   shall   also   include,   as  applicable,   all   work,   materials,   equipment,   labor   and   appurtenances   as   necessary   therefor  or incidental thereto.  (b)   The   estimated   maximum   amount   of   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   for  said improvements or purposes   is   $ 2,066,840 .  (c)   The   estimated   cost   of   said   improvements   or   purpos es   is   $ 2,170,000 ,  the   excess   amount   thereof   over   the   said   estimated   maximum   amount   of   bonds   or   notes   to  be   issued   therefor   is   the   amount   of   $ 103,160   appropriated   for   the   down   payment   for   said  improvements or   purposes   from the Township ’ s Capital Improvement Fund .  SECTION   4 .   In   the   event   the   United   States   of   America,   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of   Essex   make   a   contribution   or   grant   in   aid   to   the   Township   for  the   improvements   and   purposes   authorized   hereby   and   the   same   shall   be   received   by   the  Township   prior   to   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes   authorized   in   Section   2   hereof,   then

3  #95207920.1 168182.012  the   amount   of   such   bonds   or   notes   to   be   issued   shall   be   reduced   by   the   amount   so  received   from   the   United   States   of   America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of  Essex .   In   the   event,   however,   that   any   amount   so   contributed   or   granted   by   the   United  States   of   America,   the   State   of   New   Jersey,   and/or   the   County   of   Essex   shall   be   received  by   the   Township   after   the   issuance   of   the   bonds   or   notes   authorized   in   Section   2   hereof,  then   such   funds   shall   be   applied   to   the   payment   of   the   bonds   or   notes   so   issued   and   shall  be   used   for   no   other   purpose.   This   Section   4   shall   not   apply,   however,   with   respect   to   any  contribution   or   grant   in   aid   received   by   the   Township   as   a   result   of   using   f unds   from   this  bond ordinance as   “ matching local funds ”   to receive such contribution or grant in aid.  SECTION   5 .   All   bond   anticipation   notes   issued   hereunder   shall   mature   at  such   times   as   may   be   determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   of   the   Township   (the  “ Chief   Financial   Officer ” ),   provided   that   no   note   shall   mature   later   than   one   (1)   year   from   its  date.   The   notes   shall   bear   interest   at   such   rate   or   rates   and   be   in   such   form   as   may   be  determined   by   the   Chief   Financial   Officer.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   shall   determine   all  matters   in   connection   with   the   notes   issued   pursuant   to   this   bond   ordinance,   and   the  si gnature   of   the   Chief   Financial   Officer   upon   the   notes   shall   be   conclusive   evidence   as   to  all   such   determinations.   All   notes   issued   hereunder   may   be   renewed   from   time   to   time   in  accordance   with   the   provisions   of   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-8.1   of   the   Local   Bond   Law.   The   Chief  Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   sell   part   or   all   of   the   notes   from   time   to   time   at   a  public   or   private   sale   and   to   deliver   them   to   the   purchaser   thereof   upon   receipt   of   payment  of   the   purchase   price   and   accrued   interest   thereon   from   thei r   dates   to   the   date   of   delivery  thereof.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   directed   to   report   in   writing   to   the   governing   body   at  the   meeting   next   succeeding   the   date   when   any   sale   or   delivery   of   the   notes   pursuant   to

4  #95207920.1 168182.012  this   bond   ordinance   is   made.   Such   report   must   include   the   principal   amount,   the  description,   the   interest   rate,   the   maturity   schedule   of   the   notes   so   sold,   the   price   obtained  and the name of the purchaser.  SECTION   6 .   The   Township   hereby   certifies   that   it   has   adopted   a   capital  budget   or   a   temporary   capital   budget   of   the   Township,   as   applicable.   The   capital   budget   or  temporary   capital   budget,   as   applicable,   of   the   Township   is   hereby   amended   to   conform  with   the   provisions   of   this   bond   ordinance   to   the   extent   of   any   inconsistency   herewith.   To  the   extent   that   the   purposes   authorized   herein   are   inconsistent   with   the   adopted   capital   or  temporary   capital   budget,   a   revised   capital   or   temporary   capital   budget   has   been   filed   with  the Division of Local Government Services.  SECTION   7 .   The   following   additional   matters   are   hereby   determined,  declared, recited and stated:  ( a )   The   improvements   and   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this   bond  ordinance   are   not   current   expenses   and   are   improvements   and   purposes   which   the  Township   may   lawfully   undertake   as   general   improvements   and   no   part   of   the   cost   thereof  has been or shall be specially assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  ( b )   The   period   of   usefulness   of   said   improvements   or   purposes,  computed   on   the   basis   of   the   amount   of   obligations   authorized   for   the   improvements   set  forth   herein   and   the   reasonable   life   thereof   in   accordance   with   the   limitations   set   forth   in  the Local Bond Law, is   15   years.  ( c )   The   supplemental   debt   statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond   Law  has   been   duly   made   and   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Clerk   of   the   Township   and   a   complete  executed   duplicate   thereof   has   been   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Director   of   the   Division   of

5  #95207920.1 168182.012  Local   Government   Services,   Department   of   Community   Affairs,   State   of   New   Jersey,   and  such   statement   shows   that   the   gross   debt   of   the   Township ,   as   defined   in   the   Local   Bond  Law ,   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   bonds   or   notes   provided   for   in   this   bond  ordinance   by   $ 2,066,840   and   the   said   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance   will  be within all debt limitations prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  ( d )   An   amount   not   exceeding   $ 170,000   for   items   of   expense   listed   in   and  permitted   under   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-20   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   is   included   in   the   estimated   cost  indicated herein for the purposes or improvements herein before described.  SECTION   8 .   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged   to  the   punctual   payment   of   the   principal   of   and   the   interest   on   the   bond s   or   notes   authorized  by   this   bond   ordinance.   The   bond s   and   notes   authorized   in   this   bond   ordinance   shall   be  direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township   and ,   unless   paid   from   other   sources,   the  Township   shall   be   obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem   taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within  the   Township   for   the   payment   of   the   principal   of   the   bonds   or   notes   and   the   interest  ther eon without limitation as to rate or amount.  SECTION   9.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures   toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3  hereof   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   any   bonds   or   notes   authorized   by   this   bond  ordinance   with   the   proceeds   of   such   bonds   or   notes.   This   Section   9   is   intended   to   be   and  hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Township 's   official   intent   to   reimburse   any   expenditures  toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   describ ed   in   Section   3   hereof   to   be  incurred   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   bonds   or   notes   authorized   herein   in   accord ance  with Treasury Regulation Section   1.150-2.

The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   prepare  and   to   update   from   time   to   time   as   necessary   a   financial   disclosure   document   to   be  distributed   in   connection   with   the   sale   of   the   obligations   of   the   Township   authorized   herein  and   to   execute   such   disclosure   document   on   behalf   of   the   Township.   The   Chief   Financial  Officer   is   further   authorized   to   enter   into   the   appropriate   undertaking   to   provide   secondary  market   disclosure   on   behalf   of   the   Township   pursuant   to   Rule   15c2-12   of   the   Securities  and   Exchange   Commission   (the   "Rule")   for   the   benefit   of   holders   and   beneficial   owners   of  the   obligations   of   the   Township,   which   are   authorized   herein,   and   to   amend   such  undertaking   from   time   to   time   in   connection   with   any   change   in   law,   or   interpretation  thereof,   provided   such   undertaking   is   and   continues   to   be,   in   the   opinion   of   a   nationall y  recognized   bond   counsel,   consistent   with   the   requirements   of   the   Rule.   In   the   event   that  the   Township   fails   to   comply   with   its   undertaking,   the   Township   shall   not   be   liable   for   any  monetary   damages,   and   the   remedy   shall   be   limited   to   specific   performance   of   the  undertaking .  SECTION   11.   The   Township   covenants   to   maintain   the   exclusion   from   gross  income   under   Section   103(a)   of   the   Internal   Revenue   Code   of   1986,   as   amended,   of   the  interest on all bonds and notes issued on a tax-exempt basis under this bond   ordinance.  SECTION   12.   This   bond   ordinance   shall   take   effect   twenty   (20)   days   after  the   first publication   hereof after final adoption, as provided by the Local Bond Law.

To  the   extent   that   the   purposes   authorized   herein   are   inconsistent   with   the   adopted   capital   or  temporary   capital   budget,   a   revised   capital   or   temporary   capital   budget   has   been   filed   with  the Division of Local Government Services.  SECTION 7.   The   following   additional   matters   are   hereby   determined,  declared, recited and stated:

5  #95204632.1 168182.012  (a)   The   improvements   and   purposes   described   in   Section   3   of   this  bond   ordinance   are   not   current   expenses   and   are   improvements   and   purposes   which   the  Township   may   lawfully   undertake   as   general   improvements   and   no   part   of   the   cost   thereof  has been or shall be specially assessed on property specially benefited thereby.  (b)   The   average   period   of   usefulness   of   said   improvements   or  purposes,   computed   on   the   basis   of   the   respective   amounts   of   obligations   authorized   for  each   purpose   and   the   reasonable   life   thereof   in   accordance   with   the   limitations   set   forth   in  the Local Bond Law, is   9.61   years.  (c)   The   supplemental   debt   statement   required   by   the   Local   Bond  Law   has   been   duly   made   and   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Clerk   of   the   Township   and   a   complete  executed   duplicate   thereof   has   been   filed   in   the   Office   of   the   Director   of   the   Division   of  Local   Government   Services   in   the   Department   of   Community   Affairs   of   the   State   of   New  Jersey,   and   such   statement   shows   that   the   gross   debt   of   the   Township   as   defined   in   the  Local   Bond   Law   is   increased   by   the   authorization   of   the   bonds   or   notes   provided   for   in   this  bon d   ordinance   by   $ 605,600 .   The   obligations   authorized   by   this   bond   ordinance   will   be  within all debt limitations prescribed by said Local Bond Law.  (d)   An   amount   not   exceeding   $ 50,000   for   items   of   expense   listed   i n  and   permitted   under   N.J.S.A.   40A:2-20   of   the   Local   Bond   Law   is   included   in   the   estimated  cost indicated herein for the purposes and improvements described in Section 3 hereof.  SECTION 8.   The   full   faith   and   credit   of   the   Township   are   hereby   pledged  to   the   punctual   payment   of   the   principal   of   and   the   interest   on   the   obligations   authorized   by  this   bond   ordinance.   The   obligations   shall   be   direct,   unlimited   obligations   of   the   Township  and,   unless   paid   from   other   sources,   the   Township   shall   be   obligated   to   levy   ad   valorem

6  #95204632.1 168182.012  taxes   upon   all   the   taxable   property   within   the   Township   for   the   payment   of   the   principal   of  the obligations and the interest thereon without limitation as to rate or amount.  SECTION 9.   The   Township   reasonably   expects   to   reimburse   any  expenditures   toward   the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3  hereof   and   paid   prior   to   the   issuance   of   any   bonds   or   notes   authorized   by   this   bond  ordinance   with   the   proceeds   of   such   bonds   or   notes.   This   Section   9   is   intended   to   be   and  hereby   is   a   declaration   of   the   Township ’ s   official   intent   to   reimburse   any   expenditure   toward  the   costs   of   the   improvements   or   purposes   described   in   Section   3   hereof   to   be   incurred   and  paid   pri or   to   the   issuance   of   bonds   or   notes   authorized   herein   in   accordance   with   Treasu ry  Regulation Section 1.150-2.  SECTION 10.   The   Chief   Financial   Officer   is   hereby   authorized   to   prepare  and   to   update   from   time   to   time   as   necessary   a   financial   disclosure   document   to   be  distributed   in   connection   with   the   sale   of   the   obligations   of   the   Township   authorized   herein  and   to   execute   such   disclosure   document   on   behalf   of   the   Township.   The   Chief   Financial  Officer   is   further   authorized   to   enter   into   the   appropriate   undertaking   to   provide   secondary  market   disclosure   on   behalf   of   the   Township   pursuant   to   Rule   15c2-12   of   the   Securit ies   and  Exchange   Commission   (the   "Rule")   for   the   benefit   of   holders   and   beneficial   owners   of   the  obligations   of   the   Township,   which   are   authorized   herein,   and   to   amend   such   undertaking  from   time   to   time   in   connection   with   any   change   in   law,   or   interpretation   thereof,   provided  such   undertaking   is   and   continues   to   be,   in   the   opinion   of   a   nationally   recognized   bond  counsel,   consistent   with   the   requirements   of   the   Rule.   In   the   event   that   the   Township   fails  to   comply   with   its   undertaking,   the   Township   shall   not   be   liable   for   any   monetary   damages,  and the remedy shall be limited to specific performance of the undertaking.
